title: alignment
name: alignment
text: Alignment is typically used in the D&D game to give a starting point for characters, and deliver some help to players in determining their character's motivations, philosophy, beliefs, etc. Darkelves do not neatly fit into single alignment slots - most of the time they act as 'Lawful Evil', but they will often plan and sometimes act as 'Chaotic Evil'. This is a reliable and consistent factor among all darkelves. Darkelf morality and psyche have been outlined in the dark elf section of this wiki. Since all of the PC's and the majority of the NPC's have this same base morality, Alignment ceases to be of use for this campaign. Instead, use the dark elf section and refer to the new rules on virtues and flaws for campaign rules to help generate interesting and varied darkelf characters. Note that spells and items that use alignment are hereby eliminated; detect good/evil, alignment-based attacks, etc will not be used in this campaign.

title: ambition
name: ambition
text: Every Dark Elf has ambitions of one degree or another. Every individual and House wishes to increase its standing, its prestige and power over others. This is an undeniable and omnipresent fact about all Dark Elves.

title: Ardul
name: Ardul
text: Lt. Ardul is the right-hand officer for Captain Xulleyl . He is her trusted advisor in all things, and makes sure that everyone else knows that he is her favorite. His sneer and superiority elicit easy loathing. - Trynn's observation - A self-satisfied little sycophant, that's what he is. -Zacxh's- Cousin to The inept and *new* forman of the mines. Stole the Rubies for unknown purpose and fenced them through unknown channels. Had/has at least one mine spy in his guard unit (Jhull) does not like me.

title: artistic
name: artistic
text: Dark Elves admire things of beauty - objects, people, artistic creations, music, food and dance. In fact, no Dark Elf can reach adulthood without becoming proficient in at least one cultural art form. To fail to do this would be an insufferable humiliation to the House, and would leave the individual little better than a barbarian of a lesser race.

title: Arts
name: Arts
text: Dark elves have a rich culture, filled with aesthetic crafts and arts. It is expected that all Dark Elves will become skilled in at least one craft or art and contribute creations or skills to the betterment of their house and/or city. tea bath and spa music drumming dancing jewelry sculpture metalworking textiles

title: barbaric
name: barbaric
text: The Dark Elves are as sophisticated, artistic and cosmopolitan a society and people as any other race on earth - the closest comparison would likely be that of the burn elves of the surface. They look down on barbarism as any other civilized people would - with disdain.

title: bath and spa
name: bath_and_spa
text: The bath is a standard comfort of darkelf society. Waters are heated or drawn from a natural hot spring, scented with herbs and used for soaking tired and tense darkelf muscles. Skilled bath master s prescribe herbal mixtures and compounds for particular maladies, provide massage and tend to guests in the bath house .

title: bath house
name: bath_house
text: The bath house is a place very unlike most other parts of a darkelf community: it is lighted. Because the heat of steaming water can interfere with darksight (the steams can conceal the body heat of would-be assassins), bath houses are kept well-lighted for the safety of those inside.

title: bath master
name: bath_master
text: Similar to tea master s, Bath Masters are experts in a critical social art - in this case, relaxation (instead of civility). A Bath Master and their staff are often trained as bodyguards in addition to their more expected tasks, as guests are more vulnerable in their care than at other times. They are also in charge of the bath house .

title: Bhaerynden
name: Bhaerynden
text: Lucrative goldmine and waypoint on the way from both Nerr'tenden and T'lethtyrr to Dolthgurdar . Outpost of House Keniryn and post of Illiam . Until recently traffic from Bhaerynden had been plagued by marauders in the catacombs separating it from Llurth Dreier . Caravan traffic has seemingly returned to normal, though several conspicuous non-merchant parties from Dolthgurdar have passed through on their way to Nerr'tenden in recent weeks.

title: burn
name: burn
text: The Burn is the Dark Elven name for the surface world, inhabited by humans, Burn Elves, and others under the harsh and incendiary presence of the Sun.

title: Catacomb Bandits
name: Catacomb_Bandits
text: Band of toughs hiding out in the Catacombs between Bhaerynden and Llurth Dreier responsible for attacking several caravans bound for Dolthgurdar . According to Sharb , they were conscripted by Flikk under orders from House Orlylyl . Bhaerynden gold recovered from their hideout was melted down and used to finance their employment with House Oussund in Nerr'tenden after their defeat by the Spider Guard and recruitment by Nal . The circumstances of their loyalty to Nal remain a mystery. The Spider Guard assisted the bandits in making it easy for Orlylyl to believe they had been completely routed by ransacking the bandits' hideout but leaving a stash of House Godendar rubies for their former masters to find.

title: ChapterEight
name: ChapterEight
text: Chapter Eight: Insanity, Mages and Intrigue, as the quest for a good bottle of wine continues. He did not have to wait long. He scouted around the main chamber of Nerr’tenden looking for a nice sniper roost. The 'ceiling' of the place reached up well out of sight. hundreds of feet at least. Zacxh wondered where the giant tapering chimney terminated. Someday he would climb up there and explore. His circular route took him near the post where Anthadax the distracted Oussund mage was pacing furiously. Zacxh paused a moment wondering if he should press his luck by speaking to the mercurial wizard then approached. 'Sir, may I speak?' Zacxh was strangely drawn to this reclusive and extremely powerful elf. 'Do as you like but be quick.' Zacxh covertly looked the wizard over and saw that he was covered in ceremonial scarring. Minor wounds were scabbing all over his arms. This Ossund mage, like so many others who habitually exposed themselves to the arcane, was clearly insane. The elf seemed an unnatural blend of ability and perverse self destructiveness. To watch him was to glimpse a barely functional lunatic, always at the brink of unleashing some horrific and rare force of nature. Some would argue that arcana, by its very definition was unnatural. Zacxh did not agree. He had seen many 'unnatural' forces manifest them self through the mere power of his concentration. He felt that arcana was merely a word for those special aspects of 'nature' which only revealed them self under rare and special circumstances. Anthadax had clearly sacrificed large tracts of his soul in the exploration of those circumstances. 'Sir I hope to position myself so as to ambush any attackers which present themselves. Where should I hide so as to avoid being broiled alive by your magics?' The mage did not look at Zacxh as he spoke, and his voice was disinterested and distracted. 'I will focus on troop concentrations and the most dangerous enemies first. stay away from those.' Zacxh could see that An

title: ChapterEleven
name: ChapterEleven
text: Chapter Eleven The bag. Saner minds prevailed and the party rested for a few hours in the Mind flayer's lair. Zacxh normally would have volunteered for the first watch, but his head was pounding and his body ached in every joint. He was so mentally and emotionally exhausted by the furious mental battle he had fought with the mind flayers that he found himself slumping heavily on the ground and insensible in a restful trance before the rest of the party had even finished sorting out their gear from the fight. Zacxh was, as usual, physically uninjured by the battle, but his mind and spirit were ravaged. He had for the first time in memory found the absolute limit of his mental reserves. He sought his mental fortress to escape the ache in his bones but it was collapsed by fatigue. Zacxh had never experienced anything like it. He woke from his dreamless trance to Inarra's voice as she plyingly discussed some matter of strategy with Nal. The day was in full bloom and the walls radiated a reassuringly warm glow. The radiance illuminated the lair in stark relief and Zacxh could see clearly his surroundings. The Lair... it all came back in a flash. The battle, his fatigue, all of it returned like a recollection from childhood. Well rested, Zacxh saw the incident as a cautionary yet somehow distant event. Don't underestimate the Illithid. They are deadly. The Spiderguard formed up into their accustomed positions and moved out. Zacxh took point with Sorn and moved out to just within eyeshot. Zacxh practiced his silent movement but was nowhere as proficient in the art as the dancer with his nimble feet and absolute balance. Sorn could all but disappear at will, and Zacxh suspected that there was a bit more to it than mere stealth. The eerie little elf sometimes appeared to actually vanish when he was trying to go unseen. Zacxh wondered if that might be some latent ancestral ability which Sorn would one day discover. The voyage to Karsolithuyl went quickly and without incident.

title: ChapterFive
name: ChapterFive
text: CHAPTER 5. DINNER Zacxh pushed his legs to move faster toward the mines despite the ache and complaint of his bloated sloshing belly. He stopped for a moment, sighed then vomited violently. Beautifully prepared fish ejected with great velocity riding on a warm current of delicately spiced mushroom soup of the finest quality. Zacxh spit once to clear his palate. 'Damn shame. That was good soup.' He pressed into a short sprint to catch his trotting companions. Running was much more comfortable now. He felt foolish to have thought the captain had invited the Spiderguard for an actual dinner, and now having thought about it, it was almost predictable that his beautiful meal was laying in a ditch and he was chasing down another vile enemy of the house. That was okay with Zacxh. The dinner show had been a bit tedious anyhow. He would rather stretch his legs in pursuit of sport than watch a royal daughter of house Oussund whip her servants to death. The novelty of that sort of show had worn off for Zacxh decades ago. Trynn had looked so very concerned. It was as if he thought Zacxh might sep up to intervene. Trynn was naive in the way's of Lolth’s chosen. He thought the Priestess might have some feeling about the beating that she was taking some pleasure in it. Zacxh knew better. He knew that the priestess took no more pleasure and gave no more thought to the needless death that Zacxh gave to tying a shoe. It was just a tedious necessary chore. Even the show of fury as she hurled the bludgeoned body off the balcony. Nothing but a show. Like one of Inarra’s fits of sexual ecstasy... just a show. Zacxh moved his thoughts to the task at hand. His quarry. ...Zacxh crept down the hallway. The weak, and feinting stone shaper who had run from dinner turned out to be some sort of insurgent. He had broken into Captain Xulleyl's quarters to destroy some evidence, and was now holding out deep in the mines. Already Sorn and Inarra were looking defeated. They motioned to Zacxh the ston

title: ChapterFour
name: ChapterFour
text: CHAPTER 4. AFTERMATH Emails Inarra leaned heavily against the wall after her mental assault on the enemy captain, skin flushed and one cheek smudged prettily. She watched his face contort, and then he fell as if in slow motion, Xulleyl stepping back out of his way. Inarra's eyes snapped to the seriously injured Xulleyl, who moved to the nearest wall. Xulleyl's skin and equipment were smoldering and cracked from the fireball's detonations, her lungs heaved wetly with the ragged holes the sword had made on its passage through her body. Two more cuts bled heavily from her side and weapon arm. Xulleyl let the wall hold up her weight while she retrieved a small leathery packet from a tiny belt pouch. She opened the top and poured a fine powder into her mouth, then swallowed. Her wounds didn't appear any better, but she seemed to gain some strength from the dose. She carefully closed the packet and replaced it in the tiny belt pouch. She moved back to the fallen enemy and cleaned her blade on his cloak, then sheathed it in a single fluid motion. Standing erect, she quickly walked back down the passage, snapping an order back over her shoulder: "Bring him." She walked on without looking back. Zacxh had the dead captain draped over his shoulder as he walked down the hallway. He was visibly injured though his face betrayed no trace of pain, as always it was expressionless and bland. His leg was torn open by a wicked sword slash, and he bled from three or four other nasty cuts. Three crossbow bolts protruded from various locations; one snapped off at the shaft, presumably to allow him the movement of his right arm. His new fine cloths were torn in several locations by sword cuts that did not seem to leave any mark on the underlying flesh and every scar on his body stood up angrily. He trailed an odor of ozone and burnt hair and small wisps of smoke traced the passage of both he and his charred cargo as he limped down the corridor. The Spidergaurd rallied about their severely

title: ChapterNine
name: ChapterNine
text: Chapter Nine: Basking in the afterglow Zacxh finished his bath quickly. Certainly The meeting between Xulleyl and Valron’s aunt would conclude quickly and Zacxh did not want to upset his captain by being absent when called to duty. He toweled off quickly and began to dress when he realized how badly damaged his fine silk shirt and uniform cloak was from the fire. Zacxh borrowed a brush from the bath master and used it to scrub out and sanitize his beautiful leather armor. After a few minutes of work, the finely wrought stuff began to shine. He discarded his ruined shirt and cloak and dressed minimally in only buffed leather for his return to the barracks. A tip to the bath attendant and he was on his way, much refreshed. Something seemed missing as Zacxh trekked through the courtyard. It took only a moment to realize that the screaming had stopped. Zacxh looked to see Anthadax absently examine the bodies of two mages and then wander off in his creepy disinterested way. Zacxh moved quickly. He grabbed up the two bodies of the mages trying hard to appear as part of the general working party straightening up after the battle. He hauled the dead weight bodies effortlessly around a corner and into a discreet location to strip them of valuables. He found some interesting possibly magical trinkets for Sorn and a beautiful dagger for himself and was about to abandon the corpses when he realized one was not actually dead. Anthadax had not bothered to kill this one, none the less his destruction of the elf was complete. Zacxh looked into the elf’s eyes. There was nothing to be found there. Zacxh knew the symptom well. In his time as a slave occasionally an elf would receive the 'gift' of mercy after having endured torture beyond bearing. The mind was shattered and even if sensibility was restored, the elf would become a shattered broken soul, or worse a nightmare tortured monster hell bent on a mix of revenge and self destruction. title: ChapterOne
name: ChapterOne
text: Zacxh wasted no time leaving the arena. He knew the twisting hallways which wound out of house Ossund, but since his childhood eighty some years hence he had never followed them all the way through the door.. Zacxh reread his note. He was instructed to leave and meet a caravan but getting to the crystal gate would be no easy task. He was a secret slave of house Ossund not meant to be wandering the halls, and worse yet, he was presumed dead. Perhaps, he thought, he should try to escape via the service exit near the kitchen. He was unarmed and wearing only his soft boots and a bloodstained loincloth. Zacxh clung to seldom used corridors with his torn ears tuned to every possible source of danger. Several times he had to dive into a side chamber or hide in a tight corner to avoid discovery. By the time he reached the kitchens he was nearly shaking with tension. A few servants were working to prepare the middle meal for some house guards. They did not notice as Zacxh stole quietly into their presence. Zacxh crammed his body into a tight space between a scalding hot oven and a prep table. The oven toasted his flesh slightly as he waited and watched the comings and goings of the kitchen staff. He could not be discovered, so waited until there was a break between servings. When only one Servant remained and was immersed in the task of washing pots, Zacxh made his escape. He grabbed a sturdy looking kitchen knife, and slipped out the delivery door. Zacxh stopped outside the kitchen long enough to dig through a trash bin. He dug out an old filthy cloak and a tired leather belt into which he tucked his fine carving knife. The cloak had a hood and though ill fitting of his pit fighter's war shaped frame, it concealed his scarred and tattooed skin. He had little trouble, thus adorned, blending into the traffic leading out the crystal gate. True to the prediction of the mysterious scroll, there was a caravan in need of laborers making for Nerr'tenden . A stern and impatient look

title: ChapterSeven
name: ChapterSeven
text: An Unexpected Return Zacxh spent the morning among miners. Not many drow would relish the solo trek required outside Nerr’tenden gates to travel to the mines, but Zacxh had made the trip many times and despite the dangers of the Underdark, he enjoyed the solitude of his journey. Some of the miners remembered Zacxh from earlier encounters. He knew his reputation for destruction and violence preceded him and he traveled armed to the teeth. Despite all that, he treated them as equals and deferred to their expertise in every aspect of planning a mining operation. He had gold to spend, and he wielded it loosely buying high quality food and drink to munch upon during his endless meetings with the miners. Zacxh's goal was to gain the confidence of these highly skilled workers and promote the most competent of them to foreman for his dig. A few days of social and technical discussions would give him an idea of who was right for the job. He wanted a loyal and proficient crew. Proficiency could be bought with gold, but loyalty would have to be bought with the currency of respect. Zacxh paid it gladly. In fact he found the hardworking and dedicated miners a refreshing change in company from his grumbling and ungrateful companions. Mining is hard honest work, and for that Zacxh loved miners. The deeply scarred warrior finished a few more casual meetings and decided to head back to the barracks to see what new trouble the Spiderguard was into. As he approached the gates, it seemed something was wrong. Eight of Lt Ardul's guard were manning the gates and they visibly stiffened as Zacxh approached. He did not know why the guard was doubled or why they should be bridling at his approach. What Zacxh did know was that he would find it exceedingly difficult to bypass the security checkpoint using force. He approached. 'A bit jumpy arent you boys? Are we expecting trouble?' title: ChapterSix
name: ChapterSix
text: ChapterSix Zacxh's final Command For prologue to this chapter see posts 446 to 487 at: http://games.groups.yahoo.com/group/spiderguard/message/446 Zacxh could see from the look of shock and perhaps a touch of panic on his face that Valron had not picked up on the subtle message Zacxh was trying to send. Perhaps Zacxh's message had been too subtly hidden in double meaning. That was dangerous. Zacxh took a breath and momentarily collected his thoughts. He thought he had a captive audience, and hoped to make enough of an impact with his next few words to at last consolidate the Spiderguard into an effective unit. Perhaps he would at last bring Kadyel into the fold and end the infighting. Perhaps he would finally relieve himself of a command he could not hope to hold. Perhaps he could finally stop looking over his shoulder. Perhaps. He hoped that in the next few moments he would not be bathing in blood. The plan was risky and hinged on a lot of only loosely held assumptions. Valron and Trynn were Zacxh's two biggest concerns. Valron because he had made such an unreasonable and domineering bid for command, and Trynn because he was utterly conflicted in his loyalties. Ironically the uncertainty in their support was the very reason Zacxh could wait no longer. The clock had run out on his opportunity to deal with the only remaining obstacle to a functional Spiderguard. Kadyel. Wait any longer and Valron would usurp Zacxh's authority. Delay, and Trynn would forget his vow to follow this next and final command 'without question.' Zacxh's brief pause to collect all those thoughts was interrupted, first by a misled Nal who incredulously asked if Zacxh was foolish enough to think he could kill all forty of the elves present, and then by Kadyel who may have caught an intuitive glimpse of what was to come. Kadyel quickly and in her melodramatic way tossed yet more incitements of Zacxh's failing leadership and made a great show of renouncing her 'rightful' claim to lead the Spiderg

title: ChapterTen
name: ChapterTen
text: Heresy The entire Spiderguard except for Inarra, soon wound up back in the barracks. Zacxh watched the faces of his cohorts carefully, hoping to read their intentions more accurately this time. He fairly bubbled over with enthusiasm for Dinlin's end, but all too clearly remembered how vulnerable and alienated he had made himself the last time he tried something bold within the Spiderguard; Kadyel’s death held an important lesson for Zacxh; don't assume too much with this group. He pulled himself within his mental sanctuary and hid his enthusiasm. This time, he would hold his tongue. The discussion about Dinlin began almost right away. And as usual Zacxh was surprised by the feelings of his crew. Unbelievably, they were all of the blasphemous opinion that Dinlin must die. They all agreed that it was not only best for the Spiderguard, but the secret desire of Xulleyl and the steward as well. Unanimously, the group quickly decided that it was not a matter of whether she should be eliminated, just a matter of how and when. It was heresy and madness, the whole idea, and yet for reasons that would soon be made clear, everyone was fully onboard. Zacxh took it all in with pleased enthusiasm. Sorn disliked the Priestess's unjustified and dangerous rage. He also had an interest in protecting the elf who he was convinced might truly be a priest. Sorn had just returned from speaking to the condemned elf and found out that Rorlint came from the same destroyed house that was once his own. The house held very liberal ideas about the roles of males in society and was destroyed because of them, though at whose hands was as usual, a mystery. Sorn felt that whatever means was necessary to save his brother elf was indeed warranted, and as he was so fond of saying... He had no love of Dinlin... Predictably, Nal had much more pragmatic reasons for Dinlin's removal from power. He felt the Priestess was too unstable and zealous to have around such a small outpost as Nerr’tenden. He seemed

title: ChapterThree
name: ChapterThree
text: CHAPTER 3. REUNION Xulleyl poured over the maps looking for the possible location of the missing mage and another headquarters. A likely spot was found on one of the maps and the trio departed with haste. They backtracked to a secret door near the location of their first melee. The well hidden door was found, but search as they may, no way to open it presented itself. Frustrated, they hid themselves as best they may and resolved to wait the door out. An hour or so passed before their patience was rewarded. Sorn poked his head carefully out of the troublesome door. Captain Xulleyl took a step to announce herself, but without a look, Sorn hurled a spell. Stringy magical energy danced harmlessly around the Captain apparently causing no damage. Xulleyl pushed past Sorn who dropped horrified and prostrate to the ground. 'Nice trick' Her voice dripped with irony. The balance of the Spidergaurd were all in this larger storeroom. They looked battle worn and were in various states of repose poking through stores and what was likely loot from fallen enemies. Kadyel made a report of their battles and was sure to point out that only through her leadership and battle prowess was the group persuaded to come this far. She clearly stated that the group was disinclined to press into the mines after their first encounter. Indeed Trynn looked uncomfortable in these surroundings. Zacxh was saddened that the warrior was so skittish. He wondered if perhaps Trynn's spirit had been broken by his beatings in house Ossund just as Zacxh's mind and body had been steeled. The Captain took charge of her uncertain rabble of a unit. Zacxh stayed in the main chamber with Trynn, Inarra and Valron. Nal, Kadyel and the captain took a well beaten captive to a back room to be 'questioned' by Inarra and Nal. Apparently she had a penchant for interrogation of a gentle sort and Nal's technique was more messy. Zacxh spoke with those not involved with the questioning and together they pieced together what bo

title: ChapterTwo
name: ChapterTwo
text: By twos and threes, the remaining five members of 'The Barracks' arrived. They were: Sorn a handsome male dancer lightly armed, Inarra A gorgeous and aloof debutant with a distain for all the lesser 'Little People' in the room, Kadyel A huge tough looking female warrior, (Gill) A lightly armored and quiet male, and Trynn a shifty unarmed male slave. Introductions were made, and it was immediately apparent that the there were three classes of elf in the room. The slaves, the privileged, and the aloof. Normally, Zacxh would have gravitated to Trynn as slaves will often do, but there was something shifty and sinister about the tough looking brawler which turned Zacxh away. Sorn the dancer seemed the friendliest of the lot but plied his attentions primarily upon Innara. (Gil) was quiet and watchful and Kadyel held slaves in obvious distain and so seemed to Zacxh a typical female hard case. Zacxh surveyed the room one last time and decided that his choice of Valron was his best bet of not being killed as he rested. Hours passed, and people had felt each other out a bit when food arrived by armed guard. The atmosphere felt more like a prison than a barracks. Zacxh of course waited in a corner until all the diners had eaten, then he picked over a few scraps left on various plates. The food was fantastic. he hadn't eaten so well in years. He downed a leftover cup of water and stacked the platters and cups neatly for retrieval. The Lieutenant arrive awhile thereafter. He instructed that the troupe was to arm itself and follow to a meeting with the Captain. The party straggled out onto the hallways and were led a twisting route to a large natural cavern adorned in stalagmites with a high ceiling. The group moved in and spread out a bit. Zacxh moved away from the others a bit, sensing that they might be in a trap of some sort. When the Captain came in, that fear was confirmed. She looked to be a seasoned and tough warrior. She told the group that their identities were no longe

title: ChapterZero
name: ChapterZero
text: CHAPTER 0. THE GREAT ESCAPE “Crack!... Crack! Crack!” Lucinda’s vicious snake headed whip bit again and again. “Crack” “You ungrateful little bastard! Don’t you even have the sense to cry out. You would deprive your mistress of the pleasure of your song!!! Well, I will see to it that you sing.” “Crack!” Zacxh knew his reaction or, in this case, a lack thereof would have little effect on the outcome of this beating. He had been whipped too many times to be conned by the enraged words of a priestess. She would whip him until either he fell insensible or until she found something else to appease her cruelty. In Lucinda’s case it would probably be the former. She loved to see a male fall victim to her wrath. “Crack, Crack, Crack…” “Scream you insolent bastard!!!” A few years ago he might have even done it. He might have thought that by screaming he would appease her in some way. It could work on mistress Serinill, maybe on Mistress Theria too. But this Lucinda, well she was a real piece of work. His only defense would be his inevitable weakness. Therein lay the problem. His weakness was beginning to fail him. His body had grown large and tough it is true, but that was not it. Larger more formidable males would have collapsed by now. Zacxh had found a ‘hiding spot’ in his mind. It was strange, and in a way he knew it had been there all along, but now he could run there and take shelter at will. He used to feel a great sense of despair and loss when his body was beaten and deformed. It was just that attachment that had concealed the hiding spot from him. He now knew that his body was not related to his mind, but was simply its vehicle. Today His vehicle was going to be beaten into a bloody rag. “Crack” “Fall you insolent pig” Zacxh could hear the Snake headed whip. He could, in a detached way, feel the painful neurotoxin of the vipers' every bite. But the pain lived in his nerves and his brain. It no longer penetrated his mind. He had been whipped too many times before to be conned by the enraged words of a priestess. She would whip him until either he fell insensible or until she found something else to appease her cruelty. In Lucinda’s case it would probably be the former. She loved to see a male fall victim to her wrath. “Crack, Crack, Crack…” “Scream you insolent bastard!!!” A few years ago he might have even done it. He might have thought that by screaming he would appease her in some way. It would work on mistress Serinill, maybe on Mistress Theria too. But this Lucinda, well she was a real piece of work. His only defense would be his inevitable weakness. Therein lay the problem. His weakness was beginning to fail him. His body had grown large and tough it is true, but that was not it. Larger more formidable males would have collapsed by now. Zacxh had found a ‘hiding spot’ in his mind. It was strange, and in a way he knew it had been there all along, but now he could run there and take shelter. He used to feel a great sense of despair and loss when his body was beaten and deformed, and it was just that attachment that had concealed the hiding spot from him. He now knew that his body was not related to his mind, but was simply its vehicle. Today His vehicle was going to be beaten into a bloody rag. “Crack” “Fall you insolent pig” Zacxh could hear the Snake headed whip. He could, in a detached way, feel the painful neurotoxin of the vipers every bite. But the pain lived in his nerves and his brain. It no longer penetrated his mind. Unfortunately, that meant that his battered, branded, tattooed, and pierced frame woul

title: character background
name: character_background
text: It is important that your character have a background. The history and experiences of your character will inform the decisions you make during character creation and then during each and every session. The PC's in this game have been brought together to form an elite group called the Spider Guard . You have come from a variety of backgrounds - some possible ones might be: a noteworthy slave a captured member of another family a very junior daughter or son of the family of House Oussund a talented guard, sorceror, etc. anything else that fits in with the world as outlined in this wiki site. Feel free to put in story and character hooks for Steve to use if you wish to have more emphasis on your character during the campaign. Make sure that you specifically address some experience having to do with one or more of the personality / social factors described in the Roleplaying a dark elf PC section. Your Character Background can be as short or as long as you need it to be to address the above issues. If that means writing three sentences, so be it. If you want to write a novel describing your family's history and your tormented childhood (remember, childhood does last for decades), that's fine too.

title: character classes
name: character_classes
text: Forbidden Classes: Cleric Wizard Note that outdoor-focused powers, abilities etc have Underdark equivalents. You could easily have a Ranger, for example, skilled at traversing the wilds of the underground and being knowledgeable in the ways of fungi and mosses instead of trees. Note that Druids and Rangers would both require a bit of work, but we can create some underground version of each if a player really really wants to. Prestige Classes: Bellydancer

title: character generation
name: character_generation
text: 1. Before you begin, read role-playing 2. Next, see the Dark Elf racial traits and dark elf and dark elf PC sections. 3. Email Steve with an outline of the character you'd like to play. Once he's ok'ed your idea: 4. You need to create your character sheet. Note: for Gestalt characters, it looks like the easiest way to do this is simply print out the blank character sheets from Hero Forge and do it manually. Let me know if you need any help with it. If you're more daring (or have a single-class character) Hero Forge and Spell Forge spreadsheets or the PCGen application to create your character. Hero Forge is located in the Spider Guard Yahoo! group site (check the Monster Manual option on the first tab to enable Elf, Drow): http://games.groups.yahoo.com/group/spiderguard/files/ PCGen will be posted there soon. eTools is similar to PCGen and I'm having better luck getting the extended datasets. Try this for now. Additional character sheet options are listed on the Character Sheets page . Excel file Drow name generator: http://underwiki.redwoodrhino.com/downloads/Drow_names.xls 5. Make a cool looking character image (totally optional) (for the artistically uninclined try: http://underwiki.redwoodrhino.com/downloads/HeroMachine2.zip ) For rolling stats: Roll 4d6, drop the lowest die. Repeat five times so you have a list of six stat rolls. If you don't have at least one 16 (before racial modifications), reroll all six. Assign as you like to the various stats. Starting Equipment: Take what you think is reasonable starting equipment for your character, based on their level and background. Make sure you look at the house rules section for legal texts , virtues and flaws and other custom rules. 5. Email a copy of the proposed character sheet to Steve for approval, along with your written character background .

title: CharacterSheets
name: CharacterSheets
text: Character Sheet Resources There are a ton of character sheets out there, including user-fillable PDF forms. Some even perform calculations. Here are some of my favorites: Excel Based The Hero Forge and Spell Forge sheets on the Yahoo SpiderGuard Groups page are huge and cumbersome but they yield good results. (I've figured out how to modify them to make a gestalt rules character, and I'm happy to help out with that for your character. -Eric) Fillable PDF You need the Professional version of Adobe Acrobat to save filled-out PDF forms, but anyone with Acrobat Reader can fill one out and print it. The Hypertext d20 SRD site has a pretty modern looking PDF character sheet that performs calculations where possible. They also have an already filled-out example . The Mad Irishman has one to check out . Plain Old PDF Wizards of the Coast has a number of character sheets available , with more than a few for specific character classes. The Hypertext d20 SRD site also offers a plain-Jane, non-fillable PDF version .

title: City Council
name: City_Council
text: The most powerful eight house s of each city send their Matriarch to be part of the City Council. The Council typically strives to maintain city order (as they choose to see it) through diplomacy, intimidation, trade restrictions and military might. Note that there are no "city" resources - each Matriarch can only commit its own resources to Council activities. This can cause complications...

title: Cleric
name: Cleric
text: PC's can't be clerics in this campaign, as this is a highly restrictive class to play. Only females can be clerics, and only clerics of Lolth are sanctioned in this society. Females who do become clerics of the Spidery Queen aren't good matches for gaming because they are extremely restricted in their activities - they spend the vast majority of their time either governing and making policy decisions or keeping non-clerics in line with proper respect for Her. Clerics pretty much have their time cut out for them and that's the way it is.

title: Clues
name: Clues
text: This is a place for stray bits of knowledge and clues that you come across. From Zacxh: The old mining foreman was very trusted by the miners and knowledgable. She retired, and was replaced by an incompetent and paranoid buffoon *just* before the cave ins. (the new foreman is a cousin of Lt. Ardul) The poison in the shipment was not suited to attacking a water supply. The forecasted tunnels on the infiltrator maps lead toward the eight pointed symbol. None reach them yet. Could it be related that a large shipment of rubies recently went missing? The first contingent of gaurds that Valron and Zacxh encountered on their first foray into the mines (one day before the main party) wore an alternating mix of house Oussund uniforms and stealthy rogue type outfits. The body with no blood and a happy look on its face had no visible wounds, (or bite marks that Zacxh or Valron could find) and was discovered just outside the cave-in near the secret entrance. He was not recognized by any of the 'regular' miners and was dressed in rogue wear with a cloth-silenced shortsword. There was a spy who Zacxh and Valron brought to Captain Xulleyl. The spy was named Jhul (Like 'Jewell'). Captain X asked Zacxh to get him quickly and bring him to her. We abducted him from the chowhall and dragged him unconcious to the Captain's quarters. He was a member of Lt. Ardul's troop. (The Lt. was not pleased that we were screwing with his men without his permission). Captain X showed the spy a note (possibly the one from Sorn's pocket???) and when Jhul reacted in fear, Captain X decapitated him on the spot. We departed immediately to come find you (and kill a few dozen cave dwellers allong the way.) cave death toll (edited by Kadyel): 2 mages 1 captain 2 sergeant 2 high level scout type attendants with one of the mages. several dozen green troops (poor tactics...) title: competition
name: competition
text: Competition among darkelves is of absolutely paramount importance for individuals and the House they serve. Every darkelf house wishes to be on the City Council ; if it's already there, it wishes to be higher in prominence. While the moral code stresses darkelf cooperation for survival of the race, the Rules also specifically demand the destruction of weakness, regardless of the cost. Each individual darkelf also strives to out-maneuver peers and competitors for the choicest positions, favors and access to other leaders and opportunities. The desire for both individual and House primacy leads to intense underhanded dealings and vicious battles across every aspect of darkelf resources, from trade routes and social position to subterfuge and assassination. Houses have even been known to destroy other Houses entirely - if one House can kill all living members of a Family without witnesses from other noble families being brought to the City Council , it will go unpunished. In fact it will rise in the rankings, assuming the family was above it (and why would a House spend such resources to eliminate a weaker one?). Naturally, darkelves go to great lengths to avoid being caught in any activities that would overtly violate the Code.

title: cooperation
name: cooperation
text: Many outsiders think that the Dark Elves can't cooperate, but that is far from the truth. Each of the Chosen recognize that it is in their best interests to work together. Decades of training together and carefully planned coordination allows Dark Elf successes where more individualistic societies might fail. As a simple example, Dark Elf ambushes rarely leave survivors - every contingency has been foreseen and accounted for, with each member of the ambush party carrying out their role perfectly. This strong ability to cooperate does not preclude intense competition , which is encouraged by the third Rule of the moral code .

title: cramped spaces
name: cramped_spaces
text: When traveling in cramped natural tunnels and through narrow, low corridors that vary widely in diameter, it is difficult to move as quickly or fight as efficiently as normal. hampered combat hampered movement

title: create
name: create
text: To create a new page, simply type in the name of the page title desired surrounded as follows: ["NewPage"] The brackets and quotation marks are indicators for the wiki engine to create a new page link. Save the page's changes and view it again. You'll see a new, grey link with your page's name. Click on it. It will take you to a new page and ask if you want to create a new, empty page. Click on that link and it will open up a new page's text box for your content.

title: cruelty
name: cruelty
text: Most players believe that Dark Elves have an insatiable hunger for death, blood and cruel treatment of others. This is incorrect. Dark Elves have the capacity for cruelty, as every other race does. However, why should a Dark Elf waste time or resources with cruelty, torture, etc?

title: dancing
name: dancing
text: Darkelves excel at dance; it expresses the natural grace of all elves, and it is a hypnotic allure to darkelf audiences. Skilled dancers are at the top of darkelf artists.

title: Dark Charmer
name: Dark_Charmer
text: Members of this prestige class are suffused with charm, allure, and presence. What the world does not see however, is the need to dominate, control and manipulate everything and everyone. And should those 'delicate' tactics fail, kill that which cannot be mastered, most often by the subtle use of poison. No sneak attacks in the dark for these folks. When you die it will be looking into their eyes, confused and surprised at the betrayal, and should the charmer be good enough, maybe even glad that their radiant face was the last thing you saw. Based loosely on the Charmer prestige class http://www.hallofhero.com/PC/ViewPressClass.cfm?IDPC=61 and the Assassin Prestige class http://d20srd.org/srd/prestigeClasses/assassin.htm Level Base Attack Bonus Fort Save Ref Save Will Save Special Spells 1st +0 +0 +0 +2 Social Grace, Trustworthy, Poison Use +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 2nd +1 +0 +0 +3 How Charming +1, Immune to Charms +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 3rd +1 +1 +1 +3 Arcane Grace +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 4th +2 +1 +1 +4 Persuasive +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 5th +2 +1 +1 +4 How Charming +2, Arcane Presence +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 6th +3 +2 +2 +5 Distracting Presence +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 7th +3 +2 +2 +5 Gilded Tongue, Alluring +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 8th +4 +2 +2 +6 How Charming +3 +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 9th +4 +3 +3 +6 Speak the Truth +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 10th +5 +3 +3 +7 A True Charmer +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class Hit Die d6 Requirements To qualify to become a dark charmer, a character must fulfill all the following criteria Abilities Charisma 18+ Skills Diplomacy 8 Ranks, Intimidate 8 ranks Spells/Powers Ability to cast/manifest at least three spells from the school of enchantment/telepathy. At least one of

title: dark elf
name: dark_elf
text: The Dark Elf race is a subset of elf that lives in the caverns and tunnels of the earth. Dark Elves refer to themselves as 'Dark Elves' or 'The Chosen'; they do not use the burn epithet of 'Drow'. They are a highly organized society that is equal parts cooperation and competition . Their inherent superiority above other races has allowed them to thrive and grow at the expense of lesser beings. Naturally, this development has encouraged lesser beings to fear and hate their betters. Read more about the Dark Elf moral code . Dark Elves live for a very long time, as do their Burn cousins. Since Dark Elves don't marry and often change partners over the course of the centuries (and the fact that males die more frequently in the normal day-to-day of Dark Elf politics), they trace their lineage through the maternal side. Over millennia, this has led to a resource preference for the females - they are significantly stronger and larger than the males, and they hold the positions of social power and authority among their communities (like that of the Matriarch ). Physically, Dark Elves are beautiful, slim people who stand 4 1/2 to 5 feet tall, with only the tallest females approaching 5 1/2 feet in height. Weight is correspondingly slight and ranges from 75 to 120 pounds. Their skin ranges from dark grey to obsidian black, with hair of white or colored hue. Dark Elves have beautiful eyes of bright colored irises surrounding enormous pupils. Dark Elves are fully adapted to their unlit and harsh environment, with darksight , innate resistance to spells and an uncanny agility of body and mind that allows them to easily navigate the twisting rise and fall of tunnels and chimneys deep in the earth. See dark elf racial traits . Like their Burn cousins, Dark Elves are very aesthetically sophisticated, and dedicate a significant amount of time and energy into the Arts , crafts, and performance.

title: dark elf PC
name: dark_elf_PC
text: As with characters of every other race, the variety among Dark Elf characters and personae is limited only by the imagination of the player. What follows are some notes that should help you to understand some core Dark Elf traits as you design and think through the persona of your PC; I hope to add some depth to your perspective of this fascinating race, and change some misconceptions along the way. These should not be treated as absolutes - they are merely representative of the race as a whole. See the moral code Some Misconceptions: cruelty barbaric murderous Some Real Traits & Tensions in the Dark Elf Psyche: artistic pragmatism Despisal of weakness ambition A sense of station : Arrogance & Humility destiny & Caution cooperation & competition

title: darksight
name: darksight
text: Dark Elves can see in the complete darkness of underground passages and chambers; the range of this sight is out to 120 feet. Darksight is based in the ability to distinguish between heat and cold. Printed materials are not legible with darksight - illumination is required to write or read such things. When faced with actual illumination, Dark Elves require a short period of time (1-4 rounds) to readjust their vision before being able to see clearly. When using Darksight, they see in black and white; color is visible with illumination (typically from a candle, a lava flow or irridescent fungus).

title: DarkStarlet
name: DarkStarlet
text: Dark starlets represent the ultimate expression of the darker side of fame. They seek to use their spells and abilities to control and manipulate their audience solely to enhance their fame and dominance in life and their art. They can be haughty and arrogant to those they deem "below them" (anyone they have ever successfully intimidated or manipulated) and flattering and beguiling to their "betters" (anyone they haven't dominated yet or could improve their the starlet's status). Inspiration : "When you're a beautiful supermodel and have millions of dollars, it's almost your obligation to destroy the lives of the ugly poor people around you." Maybe a darker version of The Charmer Level Base Attack Bonus Fort Save Ref Save Will Save Special Spells 1st +0 +0 +0 +2 tbd +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 2nd +1 +0 +0 +3 tbd +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 3rd +1 +1 +1 +3 tbd +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 4th +2 +1 +1 +4 tbd? +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class 5th +2 +1 +1 +4 tbd? +1 level of existing spellcasting/manifesting class Hit Die d6 Requirements To qualify to become a dark starlet, a character must fulfill all the following criteria Feats Leadership? Class Features Distracting Presence Due to their glamorous nature and dazzling aspect, Dark Starlets gain their Charisma bonus to their AC. They retain this bonus when flat footed. Though they loose this bonus if they are concealed from the attacker or behind cover. Starlet Nature Dark Starlets gain a bonus to diplomacy skill checks equal to their class

title: destiny
name: destiny
text: Dark Elves sometimes refer to themselves as 'the Chosen'. They truly believe that their special powers and triumphant history are an indication of some divine favor, or at least a sign of primacy in the global food chain. Despite this, Dark Elves often spend an inordinate amount of time (in the perspective of shorter-lived races) planning, re-planning and plotting to account for every last possibility. They take nothing for granted.

title: Dinlin
name: Dinlin
text: Dinlin Oussund is a cleric of the Spider Queen, and has been assigned to Nerr'tenden . The residents at the outpost cower from her unpredictable, iron hand and her whip. She spends most of her time either praying to Lolth or stalking the passages and chambers away from the stronghold’s walls. When she isn’t doing one of these things, she is likely berating or whipping one of the stronghold’s inhabitants. Recently presumed deceased under mysterious circumstances, Dinlin's disappearance has sparked quite a fervor among the clergy.

title: Dolthgurdar
name: Dolthgurdar
text: Dolthgurdar is the regional capital and marketplace for a region roughly 100 miles across (as the umber hulk burrows). It is home to roughly fifty noble house s, and is headquarters for all of them. The city is managed by the City Council , whose members adjudicate disputes and make policy decisions. Its three enormous gates are open for a steady stream of trade that comes in from the entire region. The First House of the city, House Godendar , guards the city gates and oversees the enormous complexity of trade caravans, markets and disputes that come along with such an active marketplace . House Oussund is the 28th House of the city.

title: Double Agents
name: Double_Agents
text: The documents found in the mine led Captain Xulleyl to confirm that House Orlylyl appears to have been behind the invasions of the mines at Nerr'tenden . After Falt revealed Valron 's identity as an Orlylyl noble, the party decided to attempt a double-agent role with Orlylyl. Valron wrote a message to send to Valron's Mom , a High Priestess in Orlylyl. Here's what was sent: Valron's Message The party was barely able to get to Erlynden for a secret meeting with Valron's Sister , where his mother showed up, listened to the full report of happenings in the mines, instructed Valron to 'get the mineral' and then gave a bag of diamonds to pay for the operations.

title: Drezen
name: Drezen
text: Drezen is a fungus. It is the primary ingredient in the poison that is commonly used to incapacitate or render unconcious enemies of the dark elves. It's cultivation is generally a house endeavor, as the 'resources' needed to cultivate it seem to be in short supply (though specifics seldom leave the house ), this makes Drezen a moderately precious commodity. Dark elves are immune to the effects of the Drezen fungus.

title: drumming
name: drumming
text: Darkelf percussion is a marvel to hear; their acute hearing combined with darkelf precision and agility provide a vast tapestry of drumming and percussion music. From portable instruments all the way up to the large tahko drums, the darkelves love the rhythmic power and complexity of drumming. Drums are most often used as the musical accompaniment to dancing , and the audiences will sometimes get involved, adding clapping, slapping or whistling to the performance.

title: duergar
name: duergar
text: Duergar are the cave-dwelling cousins of burn dwarves. They are expert stoneworkers.

title: edit
name: edit
text: To edit a page, either double-click on the text in your browser, or click on the 'edit' link in your navigation bar on the right. It will bring you to an editable text box that you can use to add, replace or delete text. To learn more about the details of formatting, etc, go to the bottom of the front page of the UnderWiki ; there's a section of links down there about wiki.

title: Editing FAQ

title: Episodes
name: Episodes
text: The Invitation note that announced the beginning of the campaign. Very brief summaries of the Spider Guard 's adventures so far: 0. Homeward Bound in which the characters travel from Dolthgurdar to Nerr'tenden . 1. First Blood in which the characters learn about each other's abilities and have a leader selected. 2. Mines Incursion in which the characters defeat a large covert invasion of the mines at Nerr'tenden . 3. The Stoneshapers in which Stoneshaper mages visit Nerr'tenden , and one of them turns out to be an agent. 4. Illiam's Caravan in which the party brings Illiam and Keniryn gold to Llurth Dreier , dead or alive. 5. Orlylyl Family in which Valron's mother orders the Spider Guard to complete the task of the failed Miners Incursion . For more detailed narrative from Zacxh's perspective, see ZacxhBlog .

title: episodic play
name: episodic_play
text: Many games play in live, continuous action. This game will feature "episodes" that may be followed by breaks in the timeline. These breaks could last for hours, days, weeks, or even years. As the PC's complete (successfully or not) various episodes, there will likely be downtime afterwards. The downtime can be used for a variety of things - training to a new level, traveling to a new place, resuming usual activities, or even individual actions, correspondence or adventures.

title: Faer
name: Faer
text: Male apprentice bowyer at the House Oussund armory at Nerr'tenden . Of moderate build, fairly attractive, looks comfortable with his weapons.

title: family
name: family
text: The Family of a house is the actual blood-born family whose name is used by the House - these are the nobility. Only those with the House Name can be promoted to positions of real leadership in the House. The Matriarch also has the authority to grant the House Name to other individuals in the House. This is only done under extremely rare situations, and is an irreversible decision (of course, she retains the authority to kill them if need be...).

title: feats
name: feats
text: New Feats: These are taken and modified from the Forgotten Realms Underdark and the Plot & Poison sourcebooks. Armor Mastery (General) You are unusually limber when wearing a type of armor you have been trained to use. Prerequisite: Armor Proficiency of the necessary type. Benefit: Choose a kind of armor (chain shirt, scale mail, full plate, and so on). When you wear that armor, its armor check penalty is reduced by 1. ACP cannot be reduced below 0 in this way. This feat can be chosen as a Fighter bonus feat. Armor Tumbler (General) You can tumble when wearing armor that reduces your speed. Prerequisite: Armor Mastery, Armor Proficiency (heavy), Armor Proficiency (medium), Tumble 5+ ranks. Benefit: You can use the Tumble skill even when your speed is reduced by armor. If your speed is reduced by the load you carry, you still cannot use the Tumble skill. Brilliant Dancers (Dark Elf) Your dancing lights ability can range over a wider area, and you can make more of them. Prerequisite: ability to cast dancing lights as an innate ability Benefit: When you cast dancing lights , you can create three times the normal number of lights and you can have them range over a 30-foot radius area. When you cast the spell you can choose what form the lights take, in groups as though you cast three dancing lights spells. Thus, you could make four lanterns and eight spheres; four torches, four lanterns and a glowing figure; three glowing figures; or any other combination of the spell's effects. Bull Rush Strike (General) You gain an extra attack against a foe you bull rush. Prerequisite: Str 13+, Improved Bull Rush, Power Attack Benefit: Whenever you successfully bull rush an opponent, you immediately get to attack that opponent. This does not allow you to take a full attack against that foe if you have moved more than 5 feet. This feat can be chosen as a bonus Fighter feat. Cast on the Run (General) You are trained to cast spells as you move. Prerequisite: Dex 13+, Con 13+, Concentrat

title: females
name: females
text: Females are the important gender in the dark elf world. They are larger, stronger and morally superior to males .

title: First Blood
name: First_Blood
text: The PC's all meet one another and share a meal in their "guest" quarters in the Nerr'tenden stronghold. The following morning, they are brought to the arena, where they meet Captain Xulleyl for the first time. She speaks for a bit about the promise of each of them, and that assembled they will form a great weapon for the House. She soon gets down to business and the group is put into a winner-takes-all combat; in the end it comes down to Zacxh and Kadyel in a slugfest which Zacxh wins. The Captain gives him a Spider Guard pin and pronounces him leader of the group. Upon return, the group rests for the rest of the day and the evening. The following morning, Inarra confronts the group with a licentious display of a shallow cut spanning her entire back from hip to shoulder. She informs the group that she knows the perpetrator and implies that she will have a similar rendezvous with them some evening. For a detailed narrative from Zacxh's perspective, look at ChapterTwo .

title: First House
name: First_House
text: The First House of the City is responsible for keeping order, protecting the city and managing the flow of trade. House Godendar is the First House.

title: Gaullidurth
name: Gaullidurth
text: Gaullidurth is a major outpost of Dolthgurdar , owned by House Orlylyl . It mines sapphire s, but its real power comes from its strategic location, controlling access to a number of outposts.

title: Geldiirn
name: Geldiirn
text: Geldiirn'Olg Oussund is the Steward and Sword Master of Nerr'tenden . His skill with blades, loyalty and ambitions have earned him the rare honor of the House Oussund name. As Steward, Geldiirn manages the daily affairs of Nerr’tenden with competence and charm. He is good at what he does; and the fact that he handles the headaches of administration is not lost on Dinlin . Out in the remote stronghold, she lets him manage with more of a free hand than most males have. He relies on Xulleyl to coordinate guard activities to protect the hold, guard the caravans and keep watch over the slaves. She keeps the guard units on careful rotation, and it is her ambitions that have caused her to create the Spider Guard for her own uses.

title: gestalt
name: gestalt
text: Gestalt characters are outlined in the Unearthed Arcana book. The basic concept is that players can create a multi-class character that is a true combination of two classes. Each time you level, you get the benefits of both classes (e.g. spells and feats), and also take the more powerful improvement rate for saves, attacks, etc. The downside is that you will suffer a significant experience point penalty; your character will count as 3 levels higher than actual when the DM calculates combat experience point awards. You can check out the http://www.d20srd.org website for more information - use the Encounter Calculator on the right. gestalt details shows the full information on this powerful character option. Gestalt Rulings summarizes important DM Rulings on Gestalt issues.

title: gestalt details
name: gestalt_details
text: To make a 1st level Gestalt character, choose two standard D&D classes (also referred to as Core classes). You can also choose any of the variant Core classes available in the campaign. Build your character according to the guidelines below. Hit Dice Choose the larger Hit Die. For example, a monk/sorcerer would use d8 as her hit die. Base Attack Bonus Choose the better progression from the two classes. Base Saving Throw Bonuses For each save bonus (i.e. Fort, Ref, Will), choose the better progression from the two classes. For example, a 1st level gestalt fighter/wizard would have base saving throw bonuses of Fort +2, Ref +0, Will +2 -- taking the good Fort save from the fighter class and the good Will save from the wizard class. Class Skills Take the number of skill points gained per level from whichever class grants more skill points, and consider any skill on either skill list as a class skill for the gestalt character. Do not add up the amount of skill points gained from both classes, just take the higher amount. For example, a gestalt barbarian/bard would gain skill points per level equal to 6 + INT mod (and of course have four times this amount at 1st level), and can purchase skills from both the barbarian and bard lists as class skills. Class Features A gestalt character gains the class features of both classes. A 1st level gestalt rogue/cleric, for example, gets sneak attack +1d6, trapfinding, ability to cast 1st level cleric spells, and the ability to turn or rebuke undead. Class and ability based restrictions (such as arcane spell failure chance for wizards and a druid's prohibition on wearing metal armor) apply normally to a gestalt character, no matter what the other class is. Progression A gestalt character follows a similar procedure when he attains 2nd and subsequent character levels. Each time he gains a new level, he chooses two classes, takes the best aspects of each, and applies them to his characteristics. A few caveats apply, however. Class featu

title: Gestalt Rulings
name: Gestalt_Rulings
text: Gestalt Core Classes fixed for life of character: Nov. 13, 2005 The core classes you choose at level 1 to form your new gestalt class are FIXED for the life of your character. You can always choose prestige classes you qualify for at any given level and replace the related core class with the prestige class. Normal rules: the srd says that at each new level you 'choose two classes' and combine them. Theoretically you could be a fighter rogue at level 1, a thief psion at level 2, etc. This is not how we are going to use the gestalt rules. Prestige Class replaces related core class for gestalts: Nov. 13, 2005 When you choose prestige class(es) for leveling, replace the RELATED core class with the prestige class. For example, a thief-cleric choosing the assassin prestige class would advance as a cleric-assassin, not as a thief-assassin. If there is doubt about which is the more related, consult the DM. Spellcasting / Manifesting Levels don't exceed +1 / level: Nov. 13, 2005 If you are able to take a prestige class that allows you to advance "+1 level of existing spellcasting class" (or manifesting class, for psions), remember that bonuses DO NOT STACK with otherwise identical abilities. For example, if you are a psion-sorceror and take the Cerebremancer prestige class (which allows you to advance as a psion and as a spellcaster), you would have to pick whether you wish to be a psion-cerebremancer or a sorceror-cerebremancer and you would only gain 1 level of manifesting/spellcasting with each character level. You would not gain +2 levels in either one at a single character level. This is a known exploit. Note that this makes a few prestige classes irrelevant (cerebremancer among them), but that's a side effect of the power and flexibility that gestalts have over standard characters.

title: Hash'tal
name: Hash'tal
text: Hash is the rogue trainer at Nerr'tenden . She is an insightful teacher with a quiet voice, but don't let that fool you--she brooks no lapses of concentration. Her grace and agility are astounding, giving her movements a near-supernatural quality. Her body was returned to Nerr'tenden during the siege.

title: healing
name: healing
text: When you rest, add your constitution bonus to hit points regained. Rates of Healing: full 8 hours rest: 1 hp * (level + con bonus) full day of rest: 2 hp * (level + con bonus)

title: Homeward Bound
name: Homeward_Bound
text: The PC's travel from Dolthgurdar to Nerr'tenden as part of caravans. They travel as several groups or individuals, not knowing that the others have been summoned the same way. Along the way, various troubles manifest themselves, but there does appear to be a troubling event common to all three: a ruby caravan from the House Godendar mines at T'lethtyrr has been ambushed and its rubies captured. Each caravan ends up meeting an uncertain or grim fate at the end of the arduous journey, and the players are each brought to their quarters in the stronghold by Lt. Ardul .

title: house
name: house
text: A 'House' is the largest unit of social organization in dark elf society. Each House strives for dominance over its competitors in such a way that it doesn't break the moral code - or at least doesn't get caught doing so. A House Name can also be used in place of a Family member's name. For example, any Family member of House Oussund could simply be referred to as 'Oussund'. Hierarchy: Each House is ruled by the Matriarch and comprised of the family , the membership , the service and the guard . Rank: The Matriarch is absolute law; family is higher than membership is higher than service and guard Note that slaves and other Animals (like lizards, spiders and farmed water animals) are property.

title: House Aleanana
name: House_Aleanana
text: [Aleanana.gif] House Aleanana is the 20th house. They have suffered a serious blow with the sack of Karsolithuyl , but appear to still be operating extensive covert projects to raise their fortunes.

title: House Baenep
name: House_Baenep
text: [Baenep.gif] House Baenep ('Blessed by the Underdark') is the Second House of Dolthgurdar . As House Godendar is the undisputed First House , there are none below Baenep that can think of taking its #2 council seat. House Baenep has developed a reputation for itself over the millennia as a strong patron of elven culture, supporting artists and the largest Sorceror's Cadre in the region. They maintain an efficient and professional army nearly twice the size of Godendar's, and have promoted more priestesses through the ranks than any other family. For'tenden , a strategic access post and source of rare silkworms, is their most precious outpost. Other possessions include Fraxoleth , a mussels farm, and other minor outposts bringing water and food moss to market. Their outposts are nearly all concentrated in the Southern Branch.

title: House Council
name: House_Council
text: A House Council is the advisory council of senior family and sometimes membership of the House. The role of the Council is to advise the Matriarch when she wants advice.

title: House Godendar
name: House_Godendar
text: [Godendar.gif] House Godendar (the 'Dark Hearts') is the ancient First House of Dolthgurdar . As the preeminent house , they have significant responsibilities in the city and marketplace . As the keepers of the city, they take tax on all trade by all houses. Godendar wields vast influence in the region; no house controls more resources or collects more tolls than Godendar. They are significant patrons of the arts, have a powerful stable of merchants, and only House Baenep has more soldiers, priestesses and sorcerors. Godendar's coffers are reputed to be worth more than the next eight richest houses combined. In addition to its duties in Dolthgurdar , the House has several outlying possessions, including the distant carving-mushroom farms at Rhinn and the ruby mines at T'lethtyrr , an outpost very near to Nerr'tenden .

title: House Keniryn
name: House_Keniryn
text: [Keniryn.gif] House Keniryn is the 23rd house. Their family has been decimated in recent skirmishes with other Houses and even some infighting. Keniryn distinguishes itself from other Middle Houses in two principal ways: First, their Drum and Dance troupes are famous in Dolthgurdar concert halls. Second, they have fostered intense protective loyalty from their soldiers toward the noble family: Keniryn's family number only six, nearly the smallest family in any House. This makes them an attractive target for other Houses looking for a step up. Keniryn claims Bhaerynden as its outpost, a profitable gold mine distant from Dolthgurdar and in between Nerr'tenden and T'lethtyrr .

title: House Kilani
name: House_Kilani
text: [Kilani.gif] House Kilani has fallen. Rulers of T'lindeth throughout living memory, they were recently deposed and destroyed by House Kilrahel .

title: House Kilep
name: House_Kilep
text: [Kilep.gif] House Kilep ('People of the Underdark') is the 47th House. Kilep is a poor house, and its workers are focused on learning how to be efficient and capable tunnel runners, but the reality is that they are more often used as simple guards, shock troops and menial laborers by other houses. Several months ago, they suffered a serious reputation setback when two caravans they were leading to House Oussund were lost en route, along with one of their few capable captains.

title: House Kilrahel
name: House_Kilrahel
text: [Kilrahel.gif] House Kilrahel (the 'People of the Gods') has recently won the title of 13th House. House Kilrahel has toppled and apparently eradicated the long-standing rulers of T'lindeth, House Kilani . Kilrahel's power comes from their well-trained Cadre and their priestesses; both groups are specially integrated with the Kilrahel army. Kilrahel is viewed by its new hierarchy neighbors as a sort of dangerous nouveau-riche; while well-armed, their cultural accomplishments are on par with the Minor Houses - the 'Tunnel Rat' houses! The crown jewel of the Kilrahel possessions is their newly-acquired property T'lindeth , a mushroom farm with the powerful position of being the first outpost on the Southern Branch. Kilrahel also has an ancestral carving-mushroom plantation Moht'lethtyrr located far to the north, above Maeralyn .

title: House Melemtor
name: House_Melemtor
text: [Melemtor.gif] House Melemtor ('Mothers of the Abyss') is the 36th House. Melemtor is a minor House that specializes in caravan transport for other Houses, and is known for its professionalism and efficiency. The tiniest house of all Dolthgurdar , Melemtor is currently suffering under terrible leadership from its Matron; it is unclear is she is going senile or even insane. A coup from inside or outside must be imminent; the House cannot continue as it is.

title: House Noquiryn
name: House_Noquiryn
text: [Noquiryn.gif] House Noquiryn ('Sacred to History') is the 14th house. Noquiryn is a typical middle-powered house; they don't have the same across-the-board strengths and cultural achievements of Council Houses, as much of their wealth is dedicated to training what Sorcerors and Priestesses they have. Their strategically located outposts, Cloibbrath and Erlynden catch toll traffic from every outpost on the outer West Branch. These outposts are the key to Noquiryn power.

title: House Noqusek
name: House_Noqusek
text: [Noqusek.gif] House Noqusek ('Sacred to Adamantite') is the Seventh House of Dolthgurdar, holding a seat on the City Council . Noqusek is renowned for their respect for the arts, particularly the performance arts, including music and dance. The House is also very pragmatic: they are well-defended by their soldiers, and have a powerful Cadre and clergy. They own three outposts, two of significance: Llurth Dreier , a fresh mussel plantation and Dallnothax , a well-protected silver mine. These two outposts are only a few hours apart with no other outposts between them, providing significant assistance in times of trouble and also saving some toll fees on their mussels. Their third outpost is a small lumber farm.

title: House Orlylyl
name: House_Orlylyl
text: [Orlylyl.gif] House Orlylyl ('Guild of the Blade') is the 27th House. They are the only competitor for House Oussund in the sapphire trade. Orlylyl's desperate and unlikely bid to gain control of Gaullidurth several decades ago has paid off handsomely; they are now a well-respected Middle House. After its war, Orlylyl chose to focus on fewer, highly-trained forces rather than large mediocre ones. Their soldiers are renowned fighters, and Orlylyl's name has popped up more than once in rumor as the likely hand behind some well-executed operations. One of the handful of most valuable outposts, Gaullidurth is the Northern Branch gateway after Dusklyngh , and the sapphire mine there is the only one in competition with House Oussund 's Nerr'tenden . Valron has been revealed as an Orlylyl noble. Increasing evidence supports the idea that Orlylyl was behind the incursion to Nerr'tenden's mines and that they have been disrupting caravans through the Catacombs, although the fact that Oussund caravans were the only ones untouched seems confusing.

title: House Oussund
name: House_Oussund
text: [Oussund.gif] House Oussund ('Heirs to the Spider's Kiss') is the 28th House. House Oussund is one of a handful of lower Middle Houses that are in good position to further themselves in the coming years. Oussund has a thriving commitment to training Sorcerors and Priestesses, and maintains a healthy military presence. Oussund has several notable artists in Dolthgurdar , including the dancer Tal'nalthereen Oussund. The very remote sapphire mining outpost called Nerr'tenden is the family's source of income. The House's current rivals include House Orlylyl , which is the only competitor in the sapphire market, and House Keniryn , which controls a gold mine and armed checkpoint only a half-day of travel from Nerr'tenden. House Oussund caravans must pass through the gold mine checkpoint to reach the market at Dolthgurdar, and tolls are expensive.

title: house rules
name: house_rules
text: Campaign House Rules If you can find a rule in the legal texts or in these House Rules, you can use it. Note that there are House Rules that disallow certain spells, feats, etc. This website trumps the books. Note that if there's some ability or feat that really fits your character, you can type it up and add it here, with Steve's permission. House Rules Index training - disabled gestalt feats skills character classes cramped spaces psionics spells healing virtues and flaws episodic play alignment You may wish to check out the standard rules at http://www.d20srd.org . Here are the major DM Rulings for this campaign.

title: Illiam
name: Illiam
text: Understandably paranoid as a member of the small House Keniryn family, he is known for the small fortune represented by his personal armor and ornaments as well as for his skill as a dancer. Steward Illiam Kenyrin was mysteriously killed while escorting a caravan of Bhaerynden gold to Dolthgurdar under the protection of his outpost's most elite warriors as well as that of the Spider Guard during an ambush by bandits who had been harrying caravans in the catacombs between Bhaerynden and Llurth Dreier . His body was conducted by the Spider Guard the rest of the journey through the catacombs as none of the Kenyrin contingent survived the ambush. Taken to Dolthgurdar by Kenyrin forces, Illiam was ressurected by his family and has now resumed his duties in Bhaerynden . It has been rumored that Illiam has some secret liaison with Captain Xulleyl of Nerr'tenden .
