title: iocaine
name: iocaine
text: It is odorless, tasteless, dissolves instantly in liquid and is among the more deadly poisons known to dark elves.

title: jewelry
name: jewelry
text: Darkelves of both sexes often adorn themselves in a wide variety of jewelry - necklaces, rings, earrings, lip rings, nose rings, bracelets, armbands, pins, headbands, etc. Darkelves are very skilled at the various crafts required to design and make very fine jewelry of all kinds. They often prize svirfneblin gemcutters, as no race is as good at drawing the best from each and every stone as they. The only times that darkelves are not so decorated are when stealth is needed or combat is imminent. An unexpected meeting with unadorned darkelves would be very undesirable.

title: Kadyel
name: Kadyel
text: Starring Blake Tucker as Kadyel Stormbrewer [kadyel.png] She died for what she believed. For that she is blessed. Kadyel Stormbrewer came from a family surrounded with suspicions and doubts about their lives. Dark elves live a secret and unknown existence for the most part, full of plotting, scheming, and self-interest. Her family taught her as a youngster that there might be more. Childhood stories of a fantasy world often enthralled her---stories where people worked together under a common goal without killing each other when disagreements inevitably flowered. Perhaps it was the strange powers her family had that made her such a daydreamer as a child; no one spoke about them of course, and they skipped a generation anyway so were easy to overlook. But her grandmother had a way with things---she had an unnatural ability to start cookfires, tend to the sick, and her soups could never be replicated even when she wrote down every ingredient and her students followed her every instruction. But when Kadyel came of age, she was able to improve her grandmother's recipes. No one pointed it out, but when the women gathered to eat together, the rapidity of the soup's disappearance was proof enough. This contented Kadyel for a time, until outsiders noticed her talents and adolescence arrived. Then she was exposed to society's suspicions and doubts firsthand. Kadyel's teenage years were fraught with conflict, self-hatred and sublimation. She attempted to compensate for her difference by becoming a stout warrior amongst her fellow girls. Dark elves are indeed matriarchal, and Kadyel was determined to be the best warrior of her age so that no criticism could befall her. And she did well. A few were stronger than her, but none more agile. However, it was her strategic mind and strong sense of personal command that served her best. Always she was able to steer whatever group she commanded, made moreso by her newfound talent of blending in. With no powers or magic, she at last had

title: Karsolithuyl
name: Karsolithuyl
text: Karsolithuyl was until recently the seat of House Aleanana 's power. Aleanana was pursuing several significant clandestine projects all of which were apparently disrupted coincidentally during the Spider Guard 's visit to the outpost. During an audience with That Dude , Illithids raided Karsolithuyl and overcame its defense at every step. Surviving witnesses claimed that the Illithids were after information rather than simple feeding.

title: legal texts
name: legal_texts
text: For the purposes of this campaign, any feat, skill, spell, rule, etc from the Player's Handbook, Expanded Psionics Handbook or Dungeon Master's Guide is fair game. If you wish to add something in, consult the DM.

title: life at Nerr'tenden
name: life_at_Nerr'tenden
text: Inhabitants at Nerr’tenden are relatively comfortable, considering the distance from Dolthgurdar. There is a small tea house and a modest but comfortable bath and spa chamber fed from the springs. The Steward has insisted upon maintaining the amenities of civilized life, with gatherings for music , sculpture and tea . Skilled cooks provide quality meals (for the dark elves), while the bath master relaxes the mind and body of Nerr’tenden’s residents with her ministrations and scented oils. While the accommodations are not luxurious by any stretch of the imagination, Nerr’tenden is a comfortable place to plot ascendance.

title: light blindness
name: light_blindness
text: Abrupt exposure to bright light (such as sunlight or a daylight spell) blinds dark elves for 1 round. In subsequent rounds, dark elves are dazzled as long as they remain within the affected area.

title: Lolth
name: Lolth
text: Lolth is the deity of the Dark Elves - the Spider Queen.

title: Lolth's Fang
name: Lolth's_Fang
text: The dark elf monk tradition is unique and sinister. Their philosophy emphasizes personal discipline of mind and body. In fact, they have a Nietzschean super-man view of themselves and are even sometimes arrogant and disdainful towards non-monks, seen as weak and undisciplined. There is no official order, they have no monasteries, they have no leadership or hierarchy. Their philosophy of mind/body discipline is reflected in their unique fighting style , and their dedication to personal advancement is embodied by their practice of culling weak members who are not capable of advancement. There is a reason they are called Lolth's Fang. They take special pride in developing their own unique monk feats . They have an unofficial code .

title: Lolth's Fang/code
name: Lolth's_Fang/code
text: 1. Be strong 2. Cull the weak

title: Lolth's Fang/cull
name: Lolth's_Fang/cull
text: The Fang have a particularly ruthless tradition called the "cull". If you are crippled, elderly, or otherwise too weak to continue advancing in your skills, a monk will challenge you to a duel. The duel is a farce of course, since you are obviously too weak to fight effectively. The shame of being culled is great, and this keeps the monks always training rigorously to avoid such a fate. The culls are always fought with non-lethal damage for a knockout. The culled monk is now an outcast to the monks. She will never find another trainer to further her skills, and the Fang will disown her. She is dead to them. While this is a decentralized, ad-hoc enforcement mechanism ripe for abuse, it is rarely abused because the only thing more shameful than being culled is to be beaten by the one you are attempting to cull. If this happens the loser usually accepts total banishment. Some monks with failed culls have gone to the surface the shame is so great. So it is a self-enforcing tradition and works well. Culls generally only occur between monks of the same skill level as it is considered shameful to cull someone with obviously less experience than you. Although disguise *is* a typical monk skill, and dark elves *are* devious. Also, goading your enemy into culling you by feigning weakness would not be unheard of...

title: Lolth's Fang/FightingStyle
name: Lolth's_Fang/FightingStyle
text: The Fang seek to dominate their opponents through choke holds, pressure points, stun moves, and trips. Anything to keep a foe under control. While they aren't well suited to front-line combat, they make excellent assassins, bodyguards, and bounty hunters. Dark elf monks usually prefer to deal non-lethal damage. The monk "culls" are always fought using non-lethal damage.

title: Lolth's Fang/MonkFeats
name: Lolth's_Fang/MonkFeats
text: Dark elf monks value self-training and indepedence. They take special pride in developing their own unique fighting moves. Like wizards with custom spells, unique feats created by a monk are guarded closely, only passed on to trusted companions or apprentices. Even the rumour of having created a devastating new move can bring much prestige to a monk. It is said that the monk who developed the "Blinding Fist" special strike had it given to him as a gift from Lolth for some wicked service he completed for her ... There are rumors of particularly devastating feats with effects so wondrous they can only be supernaturally inspired. Only the most powerful monks and those who hire them know if the rumours are true. One such legendary move is often referred to as "I'll knock you into the middle of next week". Apparently, it can actually knock you into the middle of next week.

title: Lolth's Fang/reason
name: Lolth's_Fang/reason
text: The monks are often employed by the clerics as bodyguards, bounty hunters, and assassins. This is so well-known that it is rumoured that the monks are a secret army for the clerics, or even Lolth herself. Because of this they are often referred to "Lolth's Fang" or simply "the Fang". Some even fear that the monks may be the unwitting tools of a powerful outsider preparing an army for a sinister revolution. The monks scoff at this but sometimes even they begin to wonder where some of the darker knowledge in their special moves has come from. The clerics are too arrogant and self-satisfied to fear anyone, much less a bunch of obsessive meat pounders like the monks, so they use the monks as tools, like they do everyone else. The Fang have no special loyalty to each other and as often as not they find themselves enemies in the employ of two different House clerics.

title: males
name: males
text: Males are subordinate to females in this matriarchal culture. They are also physically smaller and morally inferior. See dark elf .

title: marketplace
name: marketplace
text: The Dolthgurdar marketplace is a dynamic and powerful confluence of goods, money and ambition. Several dozen commodity goods and crafts are traded daily at the marketplace. Caravans from the mining and farming outposts bring in enough trade to support the region's population of nearly 115,000 full-time residents and slaves. The First House guards the trade goods and oversees trade at the marketplace.

title: Matriarch
name: Matriarch
text: The female leader of a house is the Matriarch. She holds life-and-death authority over all members of the household. The Matriarchs of the top eight houses form the City Council .

title: membership
name: membership
text: The Membership of a house are the tradespeople who provide the day-to-day expertise in the House's operations; they are typically promoted to this level from the service or the guard , and hold roles of day-to-day authority in areas like trade, security, etc.

title: metalworking
name: metalworking
text: Metal is used in a wide variety of items in darkelf life - from wrought gates to swords, utensils and ornaments. Metalworking brings out the beauty in each object. Much like sculpture, this permanent record of artisan perspective is valued highly by the darkelf community.

title: Mindwitness
name: Mindwitness
text: This nightmare of eyes and lashing tentacles flies across the ground without apparent support. A mindwitness is a 6-foot-diameter orb dominated by a central eye and a small, lampreylike maw. Ten smaller eyes on stalks sprout from the top of the orb, as do four waving tentacles. It is rumored to originally be the magical cross of a Beholder and Illithid. [Mindwitness.jpg]

title: moral code
name: moral_code
text: The Dark Elves have a very strong, very simple set of core rules that have helped them to survive, and even thrive, in the hostile and challenging world they inhabit. These rules are tightly bound into the racial identity of all Dark Elves, giving them a clear sense of community, sisterhood and superiority over all others. The Rules: Do Nothing that Harms the Dark Elf Cause Serve the Dark Elf Race as You Serve Yourself Destroy or Dominate Weakness There is usually but a single punishment for violation: Death. In lenient situations (or privately judged situations), the offender might merely be enslaved or indentured . Any senior member of a Dark Elf's house has authority and is responsible for judging and meting out punishment. Only the City Council can judge a House Council .

title: mortality
name: mortality
text: Nobody likes to see their character die. However, as you go through your adventures, it is likely that you will face one peril or another; at some point, characters face their final moments. If your PC is devout and feels that bothering their deity is likely to yield aid instead of divine annoyance, the PC can call out for godly assistance - the chance of success is quite small, but it's there. For others, or for those whose deity turned away in the critical moment, here's how to think about mortality: Dark elves are long-lived and skilled with magic. They are also very pragmatic. If your superiors in your house think that you are worth more to them alive than dead than they will consider returning you to life, IF the appropriate spell is handy, IF they feel your death was undeserved and IF they feel like it. However, if you don't have the ability to repay them for their troubles, you are likely to become indentured for some period of time and/or required to complete some significant task. To Summarize: Dying is a bad thing - it's painful on the way out, you get penalized in the game, and then your life is hell when you come back. Try not to get killed.

title: murderous
name: murderous
text: Dark Elves are often assumed to be on the lookout for gleeful killing sprees at all times. This is far from the truth. A Dark Elf would not waste their time with killing unless it gained something specifically desired - killing is a lot of work and requires a lot of thought: In order for a killing to be useful, many details need to be thought through: should the body be found or not? If so, when and by whom and for what purpose and under what circumstances? If not, why not, and should the fact be discovered later or not, etc? Regardless of these, what are the next actions that should be taken?

title: music
name: music
text: Darkelves have very keen ears, and enjoy music of all kinds. Chambers may be sculpted to provide appropriate acoustics for all different kinds of musical performances. The most popular of all is drumming , although singing and instrumental music are also very common.

title: Myyrix
name: Myyrix
text: Appearance Of average height for a male, though more squarely built than the average Drow, Myyrix wears a chain shirt, well-worn leather breeches and shirt, and carries a matched set of long- and short-swords. He bears the scars expected from one working amongst the house guard. His manner seems almost distracted at times and given to tangential comments, though few believe him absent minded after working with him for even a short time. Bio (I'll fill this in as you folks actually find it out...) UNDER CONSTRUCTION

title: Nerr'tenden
name: Nerr'tenden
text: Nerr’tenden is a relatively isolated roughside stronghold about 100 miles (a week of hard travel) from the city of Dolthgurdar , where House Oussund is headquartered. (See the region al map.) The stronghold protects a small spring of clear water (which happens to be secretly collocated with one of the two known sources of Hirthal ) and serves as a base of operations for mining gems, specifically sapphires. Nerr'tenden is one of more than 50 mining or farming outposts that all support the marketplace at Dolthgurdar. There are roughly 70 dark elves in residence at Nerr’tenden at any one time, and 80-100 slaves . Approximately 60-80 of the slaves are mining laborers and haulers; their numbers vary with mortality rates and on the speed of replenishments from Dolthgurdar . The laborers are mostly duergar , with a handful of orogs in the mix. The remainder are expert miners, duergar slaves who plan and direct the efforts of the laborers. The remaining 20 slaves at Nerr’tenden are split evenly between gemcutters and household servant-slaves; the gemcutters are svirfneblin and a duergar; the household servants are dark elves who are indentured or slaves . The inhabitants of Nerr’tenden are a mix of blacksmiths, fletchers, cooks, guards and the like. life at Nerr'tenden

title: norzzen
name: norzzen
text: Norzzen moss is used to make tea of such clear and delicate flavor that it is said to be impossible to poison .

text: Poor bastard.

title: NPCs/TurNPC
name: NPCs/TurNPC
text: Tur is... was... a female tunnelrunner in charge of the caravan that brought Trynn to Nerr'Tenden Cruelly murdered by Xulleyl's men at the gates of Nerr'Tenden along with her slaves and retainers, presumably to ensure secrecy. Two of her retainers, Hurm and Drak, were small-time toughs who attempted to mug Trynn not once but twice during the trip. Trynn managed to talk his way out of it once but was finally forced into using violence to dissuade them. Acting quickly, knowing he was flanked and outnumbered, Trynn dispatched Drak down a deep hole before dealing with Hurm by knocking him unconscious. When Tur confronted them and discovered Hurm's plan to mug and/or eliminate Trynn, she threatened to leave him alone in the underdark unarmed, but relented. Later, when the caravan was slaughtered, Trynn noticed Hurm lying dead, his final act an attempt to shield Tur's body from the assassins' arrows.

title: Nzerit'man
name: Nzerit'man
text: Nzerit'man translates literally to Nzerit[in search of]'man[weakness]. It is generally manifested as a house -sponsored 'cleansing' of elements within the house that are considered or evaluated in the eyes of the Nzerman [Seeker of weakness] to be weak and unworthy of support by the house. A 'cleansing' generally results in death.

title: orogs
name: orogs
text: Orogs are essentially big orcs with some ogre blood in them. They are of average intelligence (meaning 9-12, not 'average for an orc'), and average 6 feet tall (huge compared to a dark elf ). When they can be controlled and kept in line, they make good laborers.

title: Outposts
name: Outposts
text: Dolthgurdar has many outposts, including the following: * Bhaerynden * Dusklyngh * T'lindeth * Dallnothax * Erlynden * Llurth Dreier * Nerr'tenden * T'lethtyrr * Karsolithuyl

title: poison
name: poison
text: Poisons are a common weapon in the dark elf arsenal. From common poisons like 'sleep juice' (used in ambushes, served on crossbow quarrels) to horrifically lethal or crippling toxins appropriate for ending a bloody vendetta, poisons are a weapon of choice.

title: pragmatism
name: pragmatism
text: Living in the depths of the earth is a difficult existence. The intense and continual presence of competition among the Dark Elves also reinforces the need for creative thrift. As individuals and as a race, Dark Elves have learned to be pragmatic and efficient in their every action.

title: psionics
name: psionics
text: Psionics are allowed in this game, although they are uncommon. It is important to note that we will be playing without 'transparency' between magic and psionics. This means that psionic powers do not affect magic powers, and vice versa. For example, detect psionics will not reveal magic dweomers. We will be using one portion of the much-hated first edition Psionics Handbook: Each discipline requires its own prime requisite stat - psionicists are not all Int-based characters: Clairsentience: Wisdom Metacreativity: Strength Psychokinesis: Intelligence Psychometabolism: Constitution Pschyportation: Dexterity Telepathy: Charisma Psionic characters use the prime requisite stat for the purposes of determining bonus power points and DC of manifested powers.

title: racial traits
name: racial_traits
text: These are modified from the Forgotten Realms Underdark book. Stat Adjustments: +2 Dexterity -2 Constitution +2 Intelligence +2 Charisma Immunities: sleep spells and effects Racial Bonuses: +2 v. enchantment spells and effects +2 on Will saves v. spells and spell-like abilities +2 bonus to Listen, Search and Spot checks passing within 5 feet of a concealed or secret door entitles the dark elf to a Search check to notice it as if they were actively looking for it Vision: a dark elf has darksight out to 120 feet a dark elf suffers from light blindness Spell Resistance: 11+ character level Spell-Like Abilities: A dark elf with Intelligence of 13 or higher can use the following spell-like abilities once per day (caster level = character level) dancing lights darkness faerie fire Weapon Proficiencies: A dark elf receives the following as bonus feats: Martial Weapon Proficiency (rapier) Martial Weapon Proficiency (short sword) Exotic Weapon Proficiency (hand crossbow) Automatic Languages: Undercommon Dark Elf

title: Rath
name: Rath
text: Female Tunnelrunner of House Kilep . Hard nosed leader and pragmatic, chose to trust the PCs when she needed to. Was the only survivor of the escort party for Valron, Zacxh, and Nal. Presumed executed by Lt. Ardul or his agent upon her arrival in Nerr'tenden to contain information about the Spider Guard, she was actually employed by Captain Xulleyl as a scout and operative.

title: Rauvdril
name: Rauvdril
text: The name Rauvdril means little to most dark elves. They believe that he is nothing but a character in imp's tales, a pretender to Lolth's greatness, exposed for a fraud and destroyed ages ago. Now that Valron has revealed himself to be a priest of Rauvdril, the party is learning a bit more about the god. Rauvdril is indeed a god, although one not often worshiped by dark elves. His domains of influence include Earth, Strength and Travel. There are Ten Virtues that his followers must live by - to follow these codes is to live morally, to fail at any of them is to be weak. Rauvdril’s Ten Virtues Courage – be brave, be strong, do not show fear Prowess – be excellent, be admired, be skilled, especially on the battlefield Exploration – find new paths, new places, new things Self-Reliance – don’t expect others to do your work for you Discipline – stay the course, show inner strength, focus on the goal Self-Improvement – always strengthen yourself to strengthen Rauvdril Exert your Will – shape your environment, don’t let it shape you Force is a Last Resort – save your strength for the times it is truly needed Act with Determination – when the time comes to act, be swift and strong Inspire Loyalty – impress and draw others to you by living these virtues

title: region
name: region
text: This map is a very rough overview, showing major routes and outposts of importance. Nerr'tenden is in the top center; Dolthgurdar is in the bottom right. [regional_map.jpg]

title: role-playing
name: role-playing
text: This campaign is going to be different than others you've taken part in. I want this to be a new experience for each of us. An important part of that is role-playing a character that is new to you. I imagine that few (if any) of you have ever roleplayed in a campaign comprised entirely of "evil" characters in an underground setting, so that is a start. But I want to go further. I want each and every one of you to pick a character type and personality that is new for you - that will be a challenge. It may be that you identify with the character, or that you don't. I imagine that over time, each of you will find something meaningful in your character that you can use to express yourself. Make it different. If you always play the water-spell mage, don't do it here. The same goes for personality - pick something new and interesting. This is your chance to broaden your horizons, to act as someone you never would dream of in the everyday roleplay. The gauntlet has been thrown. Have fun! Get started: The pysche and morals of a dark elf PC Dark Elf character generation and CharacterSheets

title: roughside
name: roughside
text: The darkelves refer to unshaped areas as 'roughside'. The contrast is 'inside': developed and shaped tunnels and chambers.

title: sapphire
name: sapphire
text: The sapphire market is a small one; these beautiful blue gems are a rare commodity. Only two mines send sapphires to the Dolthgurdar marketplace : Nerr'tenden and its more powerful competitor, Gaullidurth , owned by House Orlylyl .

title: sculpture
name: sculpture
text: darksight doesn't allow easy appreciation of color or two-dimensional art. Sculpture, however is very much appreciated, whether it is used to adorn buildings and staircases or as free-standing art.

title: Selbs
name: Selbs
text: Unsubtle sergeant to Commander Yaz . Selbs' devotion to her master, Illiam , seemed to outshine even that of her commander. Selbs favored intimidating spiked armor and was known for her rough, toneless voice.

title: Sergeant Ilium
name: Sergeant_Ilium
text: Initially sarcastic and uncooperative, he soon changed his attitude.

title: service
name: service
text: The Service are the staff-level people who make the house run - these are the cooks, maids, miners, laborers, slave-catchers and others who provide the services the House relies on.

title: Shar
name: Shar
text: Fastidious Armory Mistress of House Oussund at Nerr'tenden . Intrigued by taller men.

title: Sharb
name: Sharb
text: Leader by succession of the Catacomb Bandits . After their defeat at the hands of the Spider Guard , Nal recruited Sharb and her band to work in Nerr'tenden . She is currently tasked with enforcing security at the Spider Guard mining camp as they seek The Mineral .

title: skills
name: skills
text: Bathmaster (Wis) The skills of providing for guests in a bath house involve massage, washing, application of scented unguents and applying restful and healing salves. A successful DC 10 check allows for improved long-term care, increasing healing by 1 hit point per day. Use of Oil of Relaxation (see Craft Bath Oils Skill) adds +5 to the roll (in addition to their normal effects on a single subject). A successful DC 15 check provides a healthful experience, reducing fatigue by two levels and removing a number of non-lethal damage equal to the number the skill check exceeded the DC. This effort takes two hours. Use of Oils of Relaxation and Cure Minor Wounds each add +2 to the roll. A successful DC 20 check provides a very healthful experience, reducing fatigue by two levels and healing one point of regular damage for each point the skill check exceeds the DC. This effort takes two hours. Use of Oils of Relaxation and Cure Light Wounds (normal) each add +2 to the roll. Attempting to use bathmaster techniques outside of a bath house adds +10 to the DC required. Craft Bath Oils (Int) This skill allows one to create non-magical relaxing and healing oils, salves and unguents. Each attempt requires a full day of work. Attempting to create bath oils without appropriate equipment (pots for boiling water, funnels and coils for processing and extracting, etc) adds +15 to the DC required. Materials are expended whether the attempt is successful or not. Use this skill in place of Bluff, Intimidate and/or Sense Motive when performing torture. Check: To perform an i

title: slaves
name: slaves
text: Dark Elves are intelligent and like to make the most of scarce resources - they often will capture and enslave opponents rather than kill them. Typical sources of slaves are: other Dark Elves from destroyed house s or members of other races who were foolish enough to resist Dark Elf development and expansion into local areas. Slaves are kept as long as they are more productive than the cost of feeding them.

title: Sorn
name: Sorn
text: Sorn'olorrak is a strikingly beautiful young elf of uncertain lineage. Formerly a dancer of some skill, Sorn controls fell powers of shadowy darkness. Nerr'tenden artist's rendition of Sorn [Sorn'olorrak.png]

title: Spider Guard
name: Spider_Guard
text: It isn't clear what this unit is for. Xulleyl has formed the group, but has kept much of her training and plans for them a secret from others in Nerr'tenden . They are currently functioning as one of the three guard units at Nerr'tenden. The Spider Guard Roster Andrew is Nal Anita is Sergeant Valron 'dar Orlylyl Barrett is Inarra Cliff is Zaxch Eric is Sorn Nate is Trynn Sean is Myyrix Spider Guard Alumni: Gill as Len We Blake as Kadyel Stormbrewer

title: station
name: station
text: Dark Elf dealings among their own kind are often tinged with arrogance and egotism; other races see nothing but arrogance, dismissal and indifference from these powerful people. When dealing with those who are more powerful, however, Dark Elves are absolutely ready to show humility. It is a sign of respect to your betters, and increases the likelihood that they will support you in exchange for some services or relationship. Dark Elves don't live long without being sensitive to their place and station compared to those around them.

title: Steve
name: Steve
text: Steve is the gamemaster for this campaign.

title: structure
name: structure
text: Structure and Hierarchy: The City Council and the First House . What makes up a house .

title: svirfneblin
name: svirfneblin
text: Svirfneblin are the cave-dwelling cousins of burn gnomes. They are expert gemcutters.

title: T'lindeth
name: T'lindeth
text: T'lindeth is one of the two major outposts adjacent to Dolthgurdar , and as such controls the flow of goods from the south-western outposts beyond to the Dolthgurdar marketplace . The traffic is not as great or as rich as that through Dusklyngh , but control of T'lindeth has nevertheless proved highly profitable for its new stewards House Kilrahel . T'lindeth produces delicacy food items (they are the only producer of the succulent Jil'kin mushroom) in addition to being located strategically. Until they were eliminated by Kilrahel, T'lindeth was the historic center of House Kilani 's power.

title: tahko
name: tahko
text: These huge drums are made from the skins of large animals, a true rarity in the darkelf environment. Precious and treasured, the Takho drums are played only by those trained in their use. They are usually only found in city Houses of some wealth. Having a skilled Tahko drummer is a mark of prestige in darkelf society.

title: tea
name: tea
text: Tea is an important social ritual for Darkelves. It is an important part of ceremony and formality, but among close peers it is a relaxing and comfortable way to share time and companionship. Every community has a tea master and a tea house , however informal or basic. Teas are made of various mosses, lichens and mushrooms found in the tunnels, waterways and chambers of the earth. poison s are common among darkelves, but it would be an incredible affront to the darkelf society to murder or incapacitate an enemy with tea. When enemies truly cannot trust their hosts, they may take tea of the norzzen moss.

title: tea house
name: tea_house
text: Even if only a barely-adorned cubbyhole in a rugged outpost, every darkelf community has a space dedicated to the tea ceremony. The tea master reigns supreme in these places.

title: tea master
name: tea_master
text: An official Tea Master has spent some years to become certified in their trade. The Tea Master's responsibilities are critical in providing a sophisticated and civilized tea to House seniors and to outsiders. In smaller places, like Nerr'tenden , it would be more usual for a House to assign an apprentice or even a hobbyist to this role; Tea Masters can be quite costly.

title: textiles
name: textiles
text: Darkelves pay attention to their tactile sense, and clothing and carpeting are no exception. Fine silks are woven from domesticated spiders; the resulting cloth can be rough, thick, fine or smooth.

title: The Mineral
name: The_Mineral
text: Nickname for mysterious destinations marked on maps recovered from (presumably) House Orlylyl operatives in the Nerr'tenden mines. The "Mineral" chambers were recently opened by the Zaxch and found to contain overwhelming magical auras and strange caustic liquid in ceramic flasks. At least one of the two uncovered chambers binds a large number of demons. Sorn summoned The Demon with the name that will never be remembered who said that his kin had been sealed there by Illithids. It seems that the illithid incursions into the Dolthgurdar region may be intended to protect the integrity if not the secret of the chambers, and that House Aleanana may be pulling the strings of the conflicts in which the Spider Guard has become embroiled.

title: training
name: training
text: Training rules have been disabled. The Concept: In this campaign, you will need to spend training time under the tutelage of a master (who you must find and hire) in order to advance a level. In the absence of a master, you will need to spend time teaching yourself and working on bettering your techniques in solitude. You are not required to drop what you are doing and begin training once you earn enough xp to level, but don't get the benefits of a new level until you have completed the training for it. All training time (with or without a master) can be spent consecutively or broken up. Note that you continue to accrue experience points at all times; xp earned while waiting for training is not lost. You could theoretically go up more than one level in a row, but must spend the time and master fees for each level attained. The Training Process: Consult the appropriate table below for your base training time required to level up. If self-training, make Training Rolls until you succeed. Repeat for each 'class' if gestalt, if standard, you're done! Training Rolls: When self-training, you must make a Training Roll: an ability check of DC = 10 + target level; add the bonus for your prime requisite for the class in question. For example, you are self-training to level 4 as a fighter, so make a strength check of DC 14 at the end of the training period. Failed checks can be repeated each week with a cumulative +2 bonus to the check for each failed check while training a class to a new level. The DC doesn't change; the +2 bonus is added to your roll. Standard Characters: Standard Characters use the following table for determining base training time requirements to level: TrainingStandard Gestalt Characters: Gestalt Characters use the following table for determining base training time requirements for each 'class' they have incorporated into their gestalt class: TrainingGestalt

title: Trynn

text: My character is Trynn'lousha Trynn shares his thoughts and feelings on his own TrynnBlog ! Oh yeah.

title: Trynn'lousha
name: Trynn'lousha
text: Trynn is large and strong for a dark elf. He keeps to himself and stares too long when making eye contact. He moves gracefully, like a trained dancer. He wears no armor, and carries a whip, a flail, and a net. Dark elves easily recognize these as the favorite weapons of the dark elf monk tradition, known commonly as Lolth's Fang . Trynn's back and arms and legs show many scars, clearly inflicted by scourge and whip. These are the sure marks of a cleric's slave. [trynn.jpg]

title: TrynnBlog
name: TrynnBlog
text: First Entry (A quick initial reaction to my 'death') Second Entry (thoughts about my situation upon arrival at Nerr'Tenden) Third Entry (first reactions to the Spider Guard companions) Fourth Entry (Some reminders to myself about loyalty) Fifth Entry (a review of history and background leading up to my "death") Sixth Entry (our first assignment from Xulleyl: the mines part I) Seventh Entry (The assassination of Ilium) Eighth Entry (The murder of Kadyel)

title: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog1
name: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog1
text: First Entry Dear Diary. I woke up dead today. I'm outraged! My head hurts, too. Damn, I should have been a cleric. I need my mother.

title: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog2
name: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog2
text: Dear Diary, A lot has happened to me since my miserable failure on my first assignment as a bodyguard for the house clerics. My only hope is that even Shaille's death was part of the ruse but I fear that Xulleyl is far too ruthless to risk leaving her alive. Shaille is dead, it is my fault, and the Oussund priestesses will have my mother tortured to death for certain if it is ever discovered that I yet live. In fact, my failure alone may be enough that they are punishing her anyway, even as I write this. It is a daily struggle to stay put and not attempt an escape in order to return to Dolthurgar. Since the attack and my fake death I have been sequestered to Nerr'Tenden as part of some mad plot orchestrated by this Xulleyl who claims to be a Captain of the Guard for House Oussund. I have my doubts about that. Upon my arrival she ruthlessly exterminated the cargo caravan that brought me. I am certain this was done more for my benefit than for any value to be had in eliminating the caravan. She was sending a message, trying to establish fear and dominance before even meeting me. Well it worked. The callous massacre chilled me to the bone and I have no desire to cross Xulleyl. I doubt I would survive the day if I even brought her an instant of displeasure. This is the only reason I do not attempt escape. I owe it to mother to be wise and survive so that I can still save her in the future. If I were to be caught in mid-escape it would be my death. Only the future can tell where this will all lead. Signing off, By Lolth's Webs Trynn

title: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog3
name: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog3
text: Dear Diary, Xulleyl calls us her "Spider Guard" and her first command was that we all turn on each other for her amusement. She called it a "test" but what did it test other than martial skill? I found this "test" to be a mark against her competence to be honest, because martial skill is no testament to leadership and other skills may be powerful and even overwhelming but they do not shine in the kind of free-for-all she orchestrated. Given time, some of those who fell first may become the most powerful of the Spider Guard. I found her game offensive. Now I will describe my companions, as I suspect my fate is tied to them for some time to come. After witnessing the brutal massacre of the caravan that brought me to Nerr'Tenden I was blindfolded and taken to a barracks where I was unceremoniously dumped off and left to meet for the first time my new companions. I was shaken and panicked and not at my best. Valron immediately caught my attention. A large, imposing elf with a wicked-looking trident always by his side. He is an elf I would not dare to cross in battle, and he seems the type who is true to his companions above all. However, there is something sinister about him: he carries a number of vials and constantly sprinkles the contents in our food. I find this objectionable to say the least but I will not elaborate further, lest someone discover this diary. I'll just say this: he may be the one I'm looking out for but to be honest I hope he is not, as I believe Valron may be a loyal and forthright ally. I just need to clear up this business with the vials. Inarra is an imposing self-possessed beauty who clearly thinks the world is solely hers to manipulate. She may even be right but I hope she will contain herself. I can see that she could wield undue influence over me without me realizing it. Her presence and ... and breathtaking beauty are hard to ... resist. She's capable of anything. She's .... well, even the others realize the danger of letting her influence

title: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog4
name: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog4
text: Trynn, never forget that it is loyalty that has served you best in life. There may be those who see loyalty as a weakness, but it is in reality a great strength. It is because of loyalty that you even stand here today. Never forget that. Signing off, By Lolth's Sticky Web, Trynn

title: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog5
name: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog5
text: My parents were destroyed by House Oussund during a purge of traitors. They tried to take us in our sleep, and but for a loyal friend who came to warn us of the coming violence, they would have succeeded. A quick, raspy scratch outside our residence. A scrape of stone upon stone. Two scrapes. Pause. Two scrapes more. My father leaped to his feat in an instant, as if he had been waiting for that sound. He trembled visibly, whether from adrenaline or fear I could not tell. But he whispered fiercely to my mother as quiet footsteps retreated from our door. "We are betrayed!" Those words have haunted me ever since, and I am still to be convinced that he was really a traitor to House Oussund. He was betrayed. Why else would he say as much when there was no one of influence to hear it? And there is one other piece of evidence in his favor. It is said that blood runs true, and the sins and virtues of the parents are passed on to the children. But if blood runs true then my parents were no traitors, for I am loyal to House Oussund. That dark day of the purge, my parents were luckier than some others. They got a warning from a loyal friend at least. I remember my father hissing desperately at us to "Go!" and then turning to face the intruders we could already hear approaching, his rapier held ready. Unlike the clerics and nobles of the highborn, my parents held genuine affection for each other. I will never forget how they paused, how she squeezed his hand briefly before whisking me out and into the secret escape route they had planned. I've never seen a highborn so much as smile at a male but my mother stopped and burned precious seconds to squeeze his hand, knowing he was doomed. We ran. As we fled I could hear the sounds of battle, strange sounds to my young ears. The sounds of metal biting into flesh, of blood splattering a floor, the sounds of my father dying in his attempt to give us precious minutes for our escape. ... We survived for weeks. First we hid outside of Dol

title: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog6
name: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog6
text: I can only hope that my miserable display of ineptitude in the mines will go unpunished by Xulleyl. I pray she will give me another chance to show my worth. We entered with confidence. We felt cocky I think, too cocky. It became immediately apparent that they were ready for us. They knew we were coming. Perhaps they were warned by some traitor among us in the barracks. Nevertheless we started well. Despite my clumsy use of the innate darkness, we succeeded is pushing them back into the next turn of the cave. But that is where things went wrong. It was my fault yet again. The mage blinded us all because I was too slow to realize the setup. I failed Xulleyl and I failed the Spiderguard. I should have understood that the mirror was setup for a blinding spell. Curse my slow wit. By Lolth's slimy tongue I will not let it happen again. But fate saved us from my mistake in a way, although I am not convinced this newcomer is altogether a good thing for us. *Something* arrived and eliminated the remaining force, mage and all. There was nothing left. THE VERY WALLS HAD MELTED UNDER THE FIRES! It was the last straw. I wanted only to leave those mines and flee whatever demon was stalking us. But Kadyel was right, we had to finish our mission. Kadeyl... ah Kadeyl. She seemed so well-suited to a role of leadership... at first. We'll see if she learns from her mistakes because even I can see she may lack the prudence to lead us in times of high stress and danger. Elves can die in the situations we find ourselves and and playing recklessly with our lives is not good leadership. But who else can lead us? It's strange to think of being led by a male. It must be Kadyel. Who else is truly suited? Nal. Perhaps. He is not like any elf I have ever met, but his sheer force of personality cannot be ignored. Perhaps, even though he is male, he could very well act as a leader. I would have to be more convinced. I do respect his direct and forthright critiques. It feels honest rather than vind

title: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog7
name: TrynnBlog/TrynnBlog7
text: The assassination So, Xulleyl has turned us into assassins now. When we joined up with the Keniryan contingent they already mistrusted us. So much so that we marched and camped separately at their command. We were to give a "gift" to Ilium. But of course it wasn't really a gift. I'm surprised the others took so long to realize. At first they were suprised and a little confused after opening the box. But I knew what we had to do. It was obvious. I said as much and the plan took shape. Xulleyl had turned us into assassins. But to what purpose? Why kill a man who is only going to be raised in a couple days? It's obviously some kind of message. But Xulleyl's machinations are opaque to me at this time. Apparently she felt it would accomplish something valuable and that is enough. But it didn't turn out so well. I feel that once again we failed Xulleyl. It started with a botched attempt at "delivery". In retrospect there's probably little we could have done. Keniryan was so paranoid and distrustful that no matter what they would have assumed it was us. Inarra was nearly killed and barely managed to get out of range of Selbs and Yaz. At the beginning of combat I happened to be near the back with Kadyel. We heard troops approaching from behind down a narrow passage. We both decided to head them off, bottling them up. If they got through into the open who knows what they could have done. Zacxh yelled something to us as we headed down the passage but it wasn't clear. Something about "get back here". It wasn't until later that he made it *VERY* clear I had disobeyed direct orders. I pledged not to let that happen again. Zacxh showed his disappointment by striking me across the face. The poor soul only knows the language of violence. I don't hold it against him. It bothers me to see him still so warped by his past, but hopefully he can grow past it. title: Valron's Message
name: Valron's_Message
text: From Valron: Respected Noble Mother, As you must know, I have been employed for nearly 20 years as a guard in the service of House Oussund. Several months ago, my death was feigned while I was moved to a new, elite unit called the Spider Guard, and stationed in Nerr’tenden outpost. Two days ago I met an intruder, an elf who recognized me and deferred to me as a noble of House Orlylyl. He was clearly a House Member who went by the name of Falt. Seeing him has brought my past into stark relief. Although I did not recognize him (and had to pretend to misunderstand him because of my companions), this elf can vouch for my identity. Captain Xulleyl appears to be grooming our elite group for some kind of power play within House Oussund. You should know that your ‘expedition’ forces have been destroyed by the Spider Guard. Now that I have learned House Orlylyl is targeting Oussund aggressively, I cannot continue to let myself be used by her if it will mean that I must contest my own House. I offer to act as an agent for House Orlylyl to aid in the operation to exploit House Oussund's mines. I bring the other extraordinary members of the small, elite Spider Guard with me, as they are eager to work for a House with great potential status. I can arrange for the completion of the mission now as an insider, as other House Orlylyl efforts have failed. Please let the Honored Matron know that she has a new weapon. Your humble servant, Valron’dar Orlylyl

title: ValronBio
name: ValronBio
text: One-hundred and forty-one years ago, Valrón’dar was born to a Cleric mother, Qualn’dril, and metal worker father, Tluth’ididyrr in the house of Orlylyl. Not being female, Valrón learned silver smithing from his father and watched younger sister, Lael’Thara train to be a cleric under his mother’s doting tutelage. Feeling the pressure to live up to her mother’s high standards, Lael deceptively uses Valrón to practice her skills. Lael swears Valrón to secrecy that he is helping her. After years of helping his sister study, Valrón becomes relatively skilled in the ways of a Cleric and grows resentful that he will never become a priest in his house. In finding an outlet for his pent up frustration, Valrón becomes an excellent fighter and is posted to the house Guard when he comes of age. One day, Lael is given the robes of a full priestess, even though Valrón knows she could not have possibly passed her test. Valrón loses it, curses his mother and the Spider Goddess, and storms off in a rage. Valrón travels a short distance away from the city, and levitates up to his secret sanctuary. He centers himself, and for the first time prays for guidance, but not to Lolth. A strange man suddenly appears before him, introducing himself as Nal’dax. Nal’dax tells Valrón that if he prays to Rauvdril then he will help him become a full Cleric. Nal’dax tells Valrón that there is a secret movement afoot to quell the powers of Lolth by bringing in Rauvdril. Nal’dax’s job is to seek out those with the potential to learn the ways of a Cleric and who also have the charisma and talent necessary to begin spreading his word without being found out and killed. Nal’dax tells Valrón that he must train on his own under the guidance of Rauvdril and the domains of Strength and Travel. When he has become accomplished in the ways of Teleportation and Righteous Might (the 5th order) he will be visited again by Nal’dax to be taken to meet others like him in the Secret Society of Rauvdril. title: virtues and flaws
name: virtues_and_flaws
text: A number of games have Flaws and Virtues systems to help flesh out personality. Here's how it works: Characters typically select one or more Virtues and an equal amount of Flaws at character generation. An example might be choosing "dazzling wit" as a Virtue and "self-absorbed" as a Flaw. Flaws and Virtues have in-game effects. In our example, the PC might get a +1 to Diplomacy and Intimidate checks (for the Virtue) and suffer a -1 penalty to Sense Motive or Gather Information checks (for the Flaw). I like the idea and want to give the players a chance to have a bit more fun with their characters at the table. However, I don't want to add in pages and pages of Virtues and Flaws, so here's what we're going to do: Make up your own Virtue and create a Flaw that is of equal impact. Describe them and email them to Steve; when it's approved, you can add it to the house virtues and flaws section of this site.

title: weakness
name: weakness
text: Weakness is unforgiveable; to be weak is to invite destruction and betrayal. It converts close allies into scheming overthrowers. Dark Elves thoroughly root out any signs of weakness - in the world of the Dark Elves, survival of the fittest is the rule of the day.

title: Wicked Courtesan
name: Wicked_Courtesan
text: Members of this prestige class are dancing, mincing, simpering, uh ... women of the night. What the world does not see however, is the need to charm her way into your heart, uh literally. She'll try to cajole, control, manipulate or dominate you, and should those 'delicate' tactics fail, mince about and put a poisoned arrow through your heart. Take that cupid!. No sneak attacks in the dark for her. When you die it will be looking into her eyes, confused and surprised that such a cute simpering litte thing would do this to you, and should the courtesan be good enough, maybe even glad that their radiant face was the last thing you saw. Based loosely on the Scout class and the Assassin Prestige class http://d20srd.org/srd/prestigeClasses/assassin.htm , see Substitutions from Assassin Base below. title: Wizard
name: Wizard
text: There are some dark elf wizards, but dark elves seem to be naturally drawn to innate casting a la Sorceror. Being a wizard is tough for dark elves; their darksight doesn't allow them to read wizardly writing - they need to light candles to see, and nobody likes that. They also need to tote around scrolls, books, candles and flint. They're also 95% motivated by academic pursuits and research. They're not likely to go out adventuring. Besides, when it comes down to it, dark elves really are good at being sorcerors.

title: Yaz
name: Yaz
text: Commander of House Kenyrin 's elite guard at Bhaerynden and trusted bodyguard of Illiam . Imbued by her master with an intense distrust of outsiders as well as an equally fierce loyalty to him. Regarded the Spider Guard as inferiors and foes from the moment she met them. Yaz was slain along with her entire contingent during an ambush in the catacombs before she could testify as to the circumstances of her master's death.

title: ZacxhBio
name: ZacxhBio
text: “Crack!... Crack! Crack!” Lucinda’s vicious snake headed whip bit again and again. “Crack” “You ungrateful little bastard! Don’t you even have the sense to cry out. You would deprive your mistress of the pleasure of your song!!! Well, I will see to it that you sing.” “Crack!” Zacxh knew his reactions or lack thereof would have little effect on the outcome of this beating. He had been whipped too many times before to be conned by the enraged words of a priestess. She would whip him until either he fell insensible or until she found something else to appease her cruelty. In Lucinda’s case it would probably be the former. She loved to see a male fall victim to her wrath. “Crack, Crack, Crack…” “Scream you insolent bastard!!!” A few years ago he might have even done it. He might have thought that by screaming he would appease her in some way. It would work on mistress Serinill, maybe on Mistress Theria too. But this Lucinda, well she was a real piece of work. His only defense would be his inevitable weakness. Therein lay the problem. His weakness was beginning to fail him. His body had grown large and tough it is true, but that was not it. Larger more formidable males would have collapsed by now. Zacxh had found a ‘hiding spot’ in his mind. It was strange, and in a way he knew it had been there all along, but now he could run there and take shelter. He used to feel a great sense of despair and loss when his body was beaten and deformed, and it was just that attachment that had concealed the hiding spot from him. He now knew that his body was not related to his mind, but was simply its vehicle. Today His vehicle was going to be beaten into a bloody rag. “Crack” “Fall you insolent pig” Zacxh could hear the Snake headed whip. He could, in a detached way, feel the painful neurotoxin of the vipers every bite. But the pain lived in his nerves and his brain. It no longer penetrated his mind. title: Zaxch
name: Zaxch
text: Zacxh'leborin of the fallen house Leborin... is one of the most fierce looking Elves you will ever meet. He stands 5 feet tall and is dressed in rags. His strong tough body is covered in so much scar tissue that it is difficult to find any uncut flesh. The stubble on his bald head suggest that if it were allowed to grow, his hair would only poke through the managarie of ruined flesh in patches. Yet despite all this there is a handsome and confident air about the dark elf. Obviously a slave, there is a vacantly defiant cast to his purple eyes. At games start he wears a hodge podge of salvaged and bloody leather armor bits and two mismatched rapiers all covered in a dark lizardskin cloak. He never looks anyone in the eye. As a good slave should not... Conspicuously absent is the "Spidergaurd Pin." As are the orders he might cast about due to its possession. Artist's rendition of Zacxh ZacxhBlog
